name: Architecture College Platform
description:
Production-style Spring Boot microservices monorepo for an architecture faculty. Seven domain services cover identity and access, student and teacher records, the academic catalog, class scheduling, enrollments, and grades—each with its own REST API and PostgreSQL database, JWT authentication, and registration through Eureka and Spring Cloud Config.

The platform is documented and runnable as deployed: Docker Compose brings up Postgres, MongoDB, RabbitMQ, Config Server, Eureka, Spring Boot Admin, and all business services. MongoDB and RabbitMQ support enrollment preload, grade projections, and scheduling integration where asynchronous or document-oriented workloads apply.

problemStatement.problemDescription:
Combined problem descriptions from all Architecture College Platform microservices.

# account-service
Academic platforms need distinct onboarding paths per persona while sharing a single identity model.

# curriculum-service
Curriculum data is hierarchical (careers, areas, subjects) and must stay normalized, version-friendly, and easy for enrollment and scheduling to consume.

# enrollment-service
Enrollment windows mix strong consistency for seat assignment with heavy bulk preload and status tracking that should not block the OLTP path.

# grade-service
Grades must be auditable and authorizable, while students and teachers need fast, role-appropriate views of history and pending work.

# schedule-service
Timetabling ties classrooms, teachers, and enrollment capacity; updates must stay consistent and visible to enrollment and faculty workflows.

# student-service
Student profiles and progression (line, semester) must stay consistent while peer services react without tight synchronous coupling.

# teacher-service
Scheduling and grading need authoritative faculty records with safeguards against duplicate payroll identifiers and clear integration with identity tokens.
problemList: Academic platforms need distinct onboarding paths per persona while sharing a single identity model.
Curriculum data is hierarchical (careers, areas, subjects) and must stay normalized, version-friendly, and easy for enrollment and scheduling to consume.
Enrollment windows mix strong consistency for seat assignment with heavy bulk preload and status tracking that should not block the OLTP path.
Grades must be auditable and authorizable, while students and teachers need fast, role-appropriate views of history and pending work.
Timetabling ties classrooms, teachers, and enrollment capacity; updates must stay consistent and visible to enrollment and faculty workflows.
Student profiles and progression (line, semester) must stay consistent while peer services react without tight synchronous coupling.
Scheduling and grading need authoritative faculty records with safeguards against duplicate payroll identifiers and clear integration with identity tokens.

solutionList.title: Segmented signup
solutionList.description: Dedicated endpoints for student, teacher, and admin registration with role-aware payloads.

solutionList.title: Centralized login
solutionList.description: Single /login path issuing JWT tokens consumed by downstream services.

solutionList.title: Profile surface
solutionList.description: /my-profile for authenticated users without leaking cross-tenant data.

solutionList.title: Bounded REST resources
solutionList.description: Controllers map to aggregates: careers, areas, professional lines, obligatory/elective subjects, subject series.

solutionList.title: Single relational catalog
solutionList.description: Postgres curriculum_db is the source of truth; no shared tables with other services.

solutionList.title: Rich read shapes
solutionList.description: Queries by area, career, semester, and series support admin tooling and downstream services.

solutionList.title: Polyglot persistence
solutionList.description: PostgreSQL holds authoritative enrollment rows; MongoDB stores preload process state and auxiliary documents.

solutionList.title: Dedicated REST surfaces
solutionList.description: Command vs finder controllers separate writes from reads; student self-service has its own routes.

solutionList.title: Bulk preload APIs
solutionList.description: Students, subjects, schedules, and grades each expose preload + status + clear lifecycles for operators.

solutionList.title: Split controllers by audience
solutionList.description: Admin-grade queries, teacher group grading, and student self-service reads each have focused routes.

solutionList.title: Postgres + Mongo
solutionList.description: Transactional grade rows in PostgreSQL; Mongo supports projections and document-oriented reads where configured.

solutionList.title: RabbitMQ
solutionList.description: Async hooks (e.g. academic history refresh) decouple heavy updates from the request path.

solutionList.title: Dedicated REST controllers
solutionList.description: Creation, finder, update, and deletion flows are split for clarity and evolution.

solutionList.title: Capacity and roster operations
solutionList.description: Endpoints adjust spots and assign or remove teachers with explicit paths.

solutionList.title: Event-friendly runtime
solutionList.description: RabbitMQ supports notifying peers when groups or schedules change.

solutionList.title: Command vs query split
solutionList.description: StudentCommandController handles writes; StudentQueryController optimizes reads.

solutionList.title: Relational source of truth
solutionList.description: student_db stores canonical student aggregates under this service boundary.

solutionList.title: AMQP integration
solutionList.description: RabbitMQ propagates events for downstream sync (as implemented in the module).

solutionList.title: Explicit validation endpoint
solutionList.description: Pre-validate teacher account numbers before expensive downstream flows.

solutionList.title: Command vs query APIs
solutionList.description: Writes under /v1/api/teachers; reads under /teachers (legacy path — normalize at gateway).

solutionList.title: Broker-backed integration
solutionList.description: RabbitMQ keeps schedule/grade consumers loosely coupled (as implemented).

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless instances
scalabilityStrategies.description: Scale out multiple Account Service replicas behind a load balancer; no server session affinity.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Dedicated database
scalabilityStrategies.description: Tune account_db independently from curriculum or enrollment workloads.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless horizontal scale
scalabilityStrategies.description: Read-heavy catalog scales with replica count and DB read tuning.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Optional read caching later
scalabilityStrategies.description: CDN or Redis for hot GET paths (e.g. full career trees) if metrics justify it.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Scale stateless API tier
scalabilityStrategies.description: Horizontal replicas with sticky-less load balancing.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Isolate bulk preload load
scalabilityStrategies.description: Throttle or queue heavy preloads so OLTP enrollment latency stays predictable.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless HTTP tier

scalabilityStrategies.title: Tune brokers and DB pools

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API tier

scalabilityStrategies.title: Broker decoupling

scalabilityStrategies.title: Scale stateless replicas

scalabilityStrategies.title: Tune broker consumers

scalabilityStrategies.title: Rabbit consumer tuning

decisions.title: Spring Cloud Netflix (Eureka + Config)
decisions.problem: Multiple JVM services need discovery and consistent configuration.
decisions.solution: Use Eureka registration and Spring Cloud Config with config-data YAML.
decisions.outcome: Operational parity between local Compose and target deployment.
alternatives: Consul for discovery and KV config

decisions.title: Relational model for curriculum graph
decisions.problem: Careers, areas, and subjects need constraints and joins for integrity.
decisions.solution: PostgreSQL + JPA entities with explicit relationships.
decisions.outcome: Enrollment and scheduling can trust foreign keys and uniqueness in curriculum_db.
alternatives: Document store for entire catalog tree

decisions.title: Postgres + Mongo in one service
decisions.problem: Enrollments need ACID; bulk preload status needs flexible schema.
decisions.solution: JPA for enrollments; Mongo templates/repositories for process documents.
decisions.outcome: Operators get progress APIs without bloating relational tables.
alternatives: Single Postgres JSONB column for job state

decisions.title: Dual store
decisions.problem: Some reads suit documents while grades stay relational.
decisions.solution: PostgreSQL for grades; Mongo URI configured for grade_db projections.
decisions.outcome: Flexibility without giving up transactional grade rows.
alternatives: Postgres-only JSON columns for all projections
Event sourcing with separate read stack

decisions.title: Netflix Eureka + Config
decisions.problem: Platform already standardizes on Spring Cloud Netflix.
decisions.solution: Reuse Eureka and Config Server for discovery and YAML.
decisions.outcome: Operational consistency across college microservices.

decisions.title: Separate finder base path
decisions.problem: Read-heavy schedule queries should not compete with transactional endpoints.
decisions.solution: Expose /v1/api/finder/groups for lookups and slices.
decisions.outcome: Clearer caching and scaling story for reads.

decisions.title: Command/query controllers
decisions.problem: Write models differ from read traffic patterns.
decisions.solution: StudentCommandController vs StudentQueryController.
decisions.outcome: Clearer evolution of validation and query optimization.

decisions.title: Split command and query base paths
decisions.problem: Legacy read API used /teachers without version prefix.
decisions.solution: TeacherCommandController under /v1/api/teachers; TeacherQueryController under /teachers.
decisions.outcome: Backward compatibility internally; gateway should unify externally.
alternatives: Single controller with explicit versioning headers

name: Bank API
description: Minimalist modular-monolith banking REST API — IAM, double-entry accounts, transfers with idempotency, loans, append-only audit, and async notifications. Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL, Upstash Redis, and Kafka consumed from a cloud broker; observability stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki) runs on EC2.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Learning banking domains without microservice overhead
problemStatement.problemDescription: Building a credible banking backend requires double-entry ledgers, idempotent payments, loan amortization, audit trails, and secure auth — but a full microservice mesh is heavy for a portfolio or teaching project. Teams also need a path from local Docker to a real cloud deploy without rewriting the domain model.
problemList: Monolithic tutorials skip idempotency, ledger integrity, and append-only audit
JWT + RBAC + refresh rotation is often bolted on without Redis-backed revocation
Cross-module side effects (transfers → ledger → notifications) are hard to test in isolation
Local dev stacks differ from production (H2 vs PostgreSQL, in-process events vs Kafka)
Observability (metrics, structured logs, audit files) is rarely wired end-to-end

solution.solutionTitle: Modular monolith with clean boundaries and AWS-ready deploy

solutionList.title: Domain modules with hexagonal layering
solutionList.description: Nine Maven modules (shared, iam, accounts, payments, loans, audit, notifications, config, boot) — controllers stay thin; handlers own use cases; domain has zero Spring imports.

solutionList.title: Financial correctness by design
solutionList.description: Balances derived from ledger entries (never stored), BigDecimal money, transfer state machine, loan amortization schedule, and Result<T> for domain failures.

solutionList.title: Production integrations enabled in docker profile
solutionList.description: Redis for refresh tokens, JWT blocklist, payment idempotency, and token-bucket rate limits; Kafka ingress for notification dispatch when BANK_KAFKA_ENABLED=true.

solutionList.title: Deployed minimalist stack on AWS
solutionList.description: Single EC2 instance runs the app container (docker/compose.yml); RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis are external; Kafka broker is a cloud instance the app consumes from; Prometheus/Grafana/Loki for observability on EC2.

solutionList.title: OpenAPI-first API surface
solutionList.description: REST under /api/v1/* with Swagger UI at /swagger-ui.html and standardized ApiResponse envelope.

metricsList: REST API v1 under /api/v1/ (auth, accounts, payments, loans, audit, notifications)
Docker image: Eclipse Temurin 21 JRE Alpine, ZGC, healthcheck on /actuator/health
Local full stack: postgres, redis, kafka, prometheus, grafana, loki, nginx via compose.local.yml

layers.name: Presentation (API)
layers.description: REST controllers per domain module — no business logic, only DTO mapping and handler delegation.

layers.name: Application layer
layers.description: Command/query handlers orchestrate use cases, publish domain events, and coordinate transactions.

layers.name: Domain layer
layers.description: Pure Java aggregates, value objects, and port interfaces — zero Spring/JPA dependencies.
components: Transfer state machine, Loan amortization rules
responsibilities: Enforce invariants (balance from ledger, transfer states)

layers.name: Infrastructure layer
layers.description: JPA adapters, Redis stores, Kafka producers/consumers, and external notification stubs.
responsibilities: Bridge to RDS, Upstash Redis, cloud Kafka

layers.name: Data & messaging (AWS)
layers.description: External managed services — not bundled in production compose.
components: Cloud Kafka broker (notification dispatch + pipeline topics)
responsibilities: Shared state across EC2 app restarts
Decouple notification enqueue from HTTP thread

layers.name: Observability (EC2)
layers.description: External monitoring stack running alongside or on the same EC2 host.

designPatterns.title: Modular monolith
designPatterns.description: Nine Maven modules with package-level boundaries; any module extractable to microservice by swapping ApplicationEvent for Kafka/SQS.

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal (ports & adapters)
designPatterns.description: domain/port.out interfaces implemented in infrastructure/persistence/adapter — domain never imports JPA.

designPatterns.title: CQRS-lite
designPatterns.description: Separate command and query handlers with dedicated Command/Query records.

designPatterns.title: Domain events
designPatterns.description: TransferCompletedEvent, LoanDisbursedEvent, etc. in bank-shared; listeners in accounts, audit, notifications.

designPatterns.title: Result<T> for failures
designPatterns.description: Expected domain failures (INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS) return Result.failure instead of exceptions for control flow.

designPatterns.title: Idempotency key
designPatterns.description: Redis + DB dedup for payment mutations — safe retries from mobile/unstable networks.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API container
scalabilityStrategies.description: JWT + external Redis — scale horizontally with additional EC2 instances behind ALB; shared Upstash Redis for tokens and idempotency.

scalabilityStrategies.title: RDS PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: Managed backups and storage; connection pooling via HikariCP; Flyway migrations on deploy.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Kafka notification pipeline
scalabilityStrategies.description: Decouple notification dispatch from request thread; consumer group on cloud Kafka handles burst traffic.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Rate limiting fail-open
scalabilityStrategies.description: Redis token bucket protects auth and payments; configurable fail-open if Redis unreachable (logged, not silent).

securityStrategies.title: RS256 JWT + permissions
securityStrategies.description: Spring Security filter chain; @PreAuthorize on sensitive endpoints; permissions[] in JWT claims.

securityStrategies.title: Refresh rotation + blocklist
securityStrategies.description: Old refresh invalidated on rotate; logout adds jti to Redis blocklist for remaining access token TTL.

securityStrategies.title: Append-only audit
securityStrategies.description: DB trigger prevents audit record mutation; full event JSON in payload for reconstruction.

securityStrategies.title: Idempotency on writes
securityStrategies.description: Payments require Idempotency-Key; prevents duplicate transfers on client retries.

securityStrategies.title: CORS configuration
securityStrategies.description: bank.http.cors.allowed-origins from env — empty by default; set explicitly for frontends.

cacheStrategies.name: IAM refresh tokens
cacheStrategies.description: Redis key iam:refresh:{sha256(token)} → userId, TTL 7 days

cacheStrategies.name: JWT blocklist
cacheStrategies.description: Redis key blocklist:{jti} for revoked access tokens

cacheStrategies.name: Transfer idempotency
cacheStrategies.description: Cached Result<TransferResponse> per userId + idempotencyKey

cacheStrategies.name: Rate limit buckets
cacheStrategies.description: Lua token-bucket script — atomic refill + consume per IP/user key

architectureFeatures.title: Spring Modulith
architectureFeatures.description: Formal module boundaries with event-based integration; supports future extraction without package rewrites.

architectureFeatures.title: Flyway migrations
architectureFeatures.description: 15 versioned SQL migrations + repeatable seed; ddl-auto=validate in production.

architectureFeatures.title: Standard ApiResponse
architectureFeatures.description: Uniform { data, meta, errors } JSON for all REST endpoints.

architectureFeatures.title: Actuator observability
architectureFeatures.description: health, info, prometheus endpoints exposed per MANAGEMENT_ENDPOINTS_WEB_EXPOSURE_INCLUDE.

requestFlow.title: HTTP request
requestFlow.description: Client sends REST call with Bearer JWT (except public auth routes).

requestFlow.title: Security + rate limit
requestFlow.description: JwtAuthenticationFilter validates RS256 token; GlobalRateLimitFilter and @RateLimit check Redis token bucket.

requestFlow.title: Handler execution
requestFlow.description: Controller delegates to application handler; domain rules enforced; @Transactional wraps persistence.

requestFlow.title: Persistence & cache
requestFlow.description: JPA writes to RDS; Redis updated for idempotency or rate counters.

requestFlow.title: Events & response
requestFlow.description: AFTER_COMMIT listeners update ledger, audit, notifications; ApiResponse JSON returned.

eventFlow.title: Domain event published
eventFlow.description: e.g. TransferCompletedEvent after successful transfer commit.

eventFlow.title: Cross-module listeners
eventFlow.description: accounts posts ledger entries; audit appends AuditRecord; notifications builds DispatchNotificationCommand.

eventFlow.title: Kafka enqueue (AWS)
eventFlow.description: When dispatch-mode=kafka, KafkaNotificationDispatchIngress publishes to bank.notifications.dispatch topic on cloud broker.

eventFlow.title: Notification delivery
eventFlow.description: Kafka consumer processes pipeline; email/SMS sent or stubbed; status logged in notification_log table.

techDecisions.title: Modular monolith vs microservices
techDecisions.problem: Portfolio project needs credible domain boundaries without Kubernetes operational cost.
techDecisions.solution: Maven multi-module monolith with ApplicationEvent + optional Kafka; single Docker image on EC2.
techDecisions.outcome: Fast iteration locally; clean extraction path documented in .agents/architecture.md.

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL on RDS
techDecisions.problem: Financial data requires ACID, relational integrity, and append-only audit with triggers.
techDecisions.solution: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL 16; Flyway migrations; Hibernate ddl-auto=validate.
techDecisions.outcome: Production-grade persistence; same SQL dialect in local compose.local.yml.

techDecisions.title: Upstash Redis
techDecisions.problem: JWT refresh, blocklist, idempotency, and rate limits need shared state across EC2 restarts.
techDecisions.solution: External Upstash Redis — not containerized in production compose.yml.
techDecisions.outcome: Survives container redeploys; works with multiple EC2 instances behind ALB.

techDecisions.title: Cloud Kafka for notifications
techDecisions.problem: Notification dispatch should not block HTTP threads or be lost on app restart.
techDecisions.solution: Kafka broker on cloud instance; BANK_KAFKA_ENABLED=true and dispatch-mode=kafka in docker profile.
techDecisions.outcome: Same code path testable locally via compose.local.yml kafka service.

techDecisions.title: EC2 observability stack
techDecisions.problem: Need metrics and logs without managed CloudWatch-only setup for portfolio demo.
techDecisions.solution: Prometheus scrapes app on EC2; Grafana dashboards; Loki for log aggregation; optional ELK profile.
techDecisions.outcome: Full visibility documented in docker/MONITORING.md and docs/OBSERVABILITY.md.

codeExamples.title: Standardized API response envelope
codeExamples.description: All REST endpoints return ApiResponse<T> with data, meta.timestamp, and errors[] for logical failures.

files.name: ApiResponse.java
files.explanation: Success and failure factories keep controller responses consistent across modules.

codeExamples.title: Transfer initiation with idempotency
codeExamples.description: Payments check Redis cache and DB before processing — duplicate Idempotency-Key returns cached Result.

files.name: InitiateTransferHandler.java
files.explanation: Idempotency-Key header is required; self-transfer and currency rules enforced before state transition.

codeExamples.title: Redis token-bucket rate limiter
codeExamples.description: Atomic Lua script refill + consume in one round trip; fail-open when Redis errors if configured.

files.name: RedisTokenBucketRateLimiter.java
files.explanation: Used by GlobalRateLimitFilter and AnnotatedRateLimitInterceptor in docker/AWS profile.

codeExamples.title: Kafka notification dispatch ingress
codeExamples.description: When dispatch-mode=kafka, notifications are enqueued to cloud broker instead of processed inline.

files.name: KafkaNotificationDispatchIngress.java
files.explanation: Active only when bank.notifications.dispatch-mode=kafka — matches AWS deploy configuration.

codeExamples.title: Money value object
codeExamples.description: Immutable currency-aware amount with BigDecimal scale 2 — used everywhere financial math is needed.

files.name: Money.java
files.explanation: Compact constructor rejects negative amounts and normalizes scale — never use double for money.

features.title: JWT authentication & RBAC
features.description: RS256 JWT with roles (CUSTOMER, ADMIN, AUDITOR) and fine-grained permissions. Refresh tokens stored in Upstash Redis with rotation on refresh; jti blocklist on logout.
highlights: Access TTL 15 min, refresh TTL 7 days
Redis-backed refresh store enabled in docker/AWS profile

features.title: Double-entry ledger accounts
features.description: CHECKING, SAVINGS, and LOAN accounts. Balance always computed from ledger entries — never stored as a mutable field.
highlights: Ledger entries on transfers, loan disbursement, repayments

features.title: Transfers with idempotency
features.description: PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED state machine. Redis idempotency cache (24h) plus DB dedup. Publishes TransferCompletedEvent for ledger and notifications.
highlights: Result<T> pattern for domain errors → HTTP 422

features.title: Loan origination & amortization
features.description: Fixed monthly payment schedule at origination. Approval creates LOAN account and disburses to checking. Repayments debit checking via domain events.

features.title: Append-only audit trail
features.description: Every BankDomainEvent captured as immutable AuditRecord (JSONB payload). DB trigger prevents UPDATE/DELETE.
highlights: AuditEventListener subscribes to all domain events
UUID v7 time-ordered IDs for range queries

features.title: Async notifications (direct + Kafka)
features.description: Fire-and-forget email/SMS on transfer and loan events. In AWS/docker profile, dispatch-mode=kafka enqueues to cloud Kafka; consumer in bank-notifications processes pipeline.
highlights: Dev stubs log to console; production uses SMTP/SMS config

features.title: Redis token-bucket rate limiting
features.description: Global filter + @RateLimit annotation on controllers. Lua script for atomic refill/consume. Fail-open on Redis errors when configured.

features.title: OpenAPI & standardized responses
features.description: springdoc-openapi with @BankApiOperation annotations. ApiResponse<T> envelope with data, meta.timestamp, and errors[].

features.title: Docker + AWS production deploy
features.description: Multi-stage Temurin 21 JRE image (~300MB). compose.yml runs app only on EC2; RDS, Upstash Redis, and cloud Kafka via .env. Observability on EC2.
highlights: docker/compose.yml — single app container on EC2

metrics.description: docker/compose.yml publishes API port on EC2 (security group)

dockerFiles.description: Single app service — Postgres, Redis, and Kafka are external cloud instances configured via repo-root .env.
dockerFiles.content:
name: bank-api
services:
  app:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile
    image: bank-api:${BANK_API_IMAGE_TAG}
    env_file:
      - ../.env
    environment:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: docker
    ports:
      - "${APP_HTTP_PORT}:8080"
    restart: unless-stopped
# External: RDS PostgreSQL + Upstash Redis + Cloud Kafka
# Observability: Prometheus/Grafana/Loki on EC2 (see docker/MONITORING.md)

dockerFiles.description: JDK 21 Alpine builder runs mvn package; JRE 21 Alpine runtime runs as non-root bank user.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine AS builder
RUN ./mvnw -pl bank-boot -am package -DskipTests
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine
USER bank
EXPOSE 8080
ENV JAVA_OPTS="-XX:+UseZGC"
HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8080/actuator/health
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "exec java $JAVA_OPTS -jar /app/app.jar"]

dockerFiles.description: postgres, redis, kafka, prometheus, grafana, loki, promtail, nginx, app — for local integration testing.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  postgres:   # postgres:16-alpine
  redis:      # redis:7-alpine
  kafka:      # confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0
  prometheus: # scrapes app:8080/actuator/prometheus
  grafana:    # dashboards + Loki datasource
  loki:       # log aggregation

name: Cinema Platform
description:
Cinema Platform is a distributed backend for cinema operations, built as nine FastAPI microservices that share Clean Architecture, async PostgreSQL, Redis, and optional Kafka event streaming. It covers the full customer and staff journey: catalog and showtimes, ticketing, payments, digital wallets, concessions, identity, workforce management, and multi-channel notifications.

Services communicate over REST and gRPC with JWT role-based access control. Local development uses Docker Compose; production targets AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis.

problemStatement.problemDescription:
Combined problem descriptions from all Cinema Platform services.

# billboard-service
Cinema platforms need a centralized service to schedule showtimes across theaters,
track seat availability in real time, enforce booking business rules, and coordinate
with catalog and payment services. Without a dedicated billboard service, scheduling
logic leaks into multiple apps, seat state becomes inconsistent, and integrations
are brittle.

# catalog-service
Cinema platforms need a single source of truth for movies, cinema locations,
theater screens, and seat layouts. Downstream services (billboard, ticket, payment)
require fast, consistent catalog lookups — often via gRPC — while admins need
rich CRUD with business-rule enforcement and audit-friendly soft deletes.

# concession-service
Theaters need a unified backend for concession inventory — snacks, beverages,
combos, and time-limited promotions — with low-latency reads for POS systems
and integration with ticketing/loyalty microservices. Monolithic catalogs lack
flexible pricing, promotion rules, and protocol choice (REST vs gRPC).

# employee-service
Cinema chains need a dedicated service to track staff across departments (projection,
concessions, ticketing, security, etc.), manage shift schedules, and handle vacation
approvals. Without a centralized employee service, HR data fragments across spreadsheets
and ad-hoc tools, schedule conflicts go undetected, and authorization for workforce
APIs becomes inconsistent across the platform.

# notification-service
Each Cinema Platform microservice historically handled its own emails, SMS, and
in-app alerts. That leads to inconsistent templates, scattered SMTP/Twilio credentials,
duplicate deliveries on retried Kafka events, and poor operational visibility when
notifications fail.

# payment-service
Cinema purchases span tickets, concessions, merchandise, subscriptions, and wallet
top-ups across multiple services. A dedicated payment service must orchestrate
provider integration (Stripe), enforce refund rules, emit events, and expose
role-appropriate APIs while keeping PCI-sensitive data off the platform database.

# ticket-service
Cinema platforms need reliable ticket purchasing, seat reservations, and lifecycle
management while integrating with billboard showtimes and payment authorization.
High concurrency requires atomic seat handling with authoritative validation.

# user-service
Cinema platforms need centralized user management for registration, authentication,
profiles, and authorization across microservices. Legacy systems often lack secure
session management, MFA, and event-driven lifecycle notifications.

# wallet-service
Cinema platforms need a centralized wallet service so customers can preload
credit, pay for tickets and concessions, and review transaction history.
Without a dedicated wallet bounded context, payment logic leaks into order
and user services, balances become inconsistent, and financial audit trails
are incomplete.

solutionList.title: Showtime Domain Model
solutionList.description: Rich domain entity with encapsulated validation for price ($3–$50), duration (30–300 min), schedule window, and lifecycle transitions.

solutionList.title: Seat Reservation System
solutionList.description: Per-seat take/leave operations with timestamps, user IDs, and transaction tracking; available-seat counts derived at the showtime level.

solutionList.title: Catalog Service Integration
solutionList.description: gRPC port/adapter to validate movie and theater existence and fetch theater capacity before persisting showtimes.

solutionList.title: Async Infrastructure
solutionList.description: Async SQLAlchemy + Redis caching + optional Kafka publisher, all wired through FastAPI dependency injection and use-case containers.

solutionList.title: Multi-Domain Architecture
solutionList.description: Separate bounded contexts for Movies, Cinemas, Theaters, and Seats with shared infrastructure (auth, pagination, events, persistence).

solutionList.title: Domain Business Rules
solutionList.description: Theater capacity validation by type, movie exhibition date logic, maintenance mode, and seat type classification in domain entities.

solutionList.title: Dual API Surface
solutionList.description: REST for admin/portal operations; embedded gRPC server for sub-30 ms catalog queries from peer microservices.

solutionList.title: Soft Delete Pattern
solutionList.description: deleted_at on all catalog entities with explicit restore endpoints for cinemas and theaters.

solutionList.title: Hexagonal Architecture
solutionList.description: Domain logic isolated from SQLAlchemy, FastAPI, and Redis via ports/adapters; testable use cases per bounded context.

solutionList.title: Dual Protocol (REST + gRPC)
solutionList.description: REST `/api/v2/*` for external clients; ConcessionCatalogService gRPC for bulk product/combo/promotion lookups by ID.

solutionList.title: Redis Caching Layer
solutionList.description: init_cache() on FastAPI lifespan for sub-100 ms hot reads and reduced DB load.

solutionList.title: CQRS Use Cases
solutionList.description: Separate commands (create/update/delete) and queries (search, get-by-id) with pagination across all list endpoints.

solutionList.title: Employee Domain Model
solutionList.description: Rich `Employee` entity with state transitions (terminate, suspend, reactivate, promote) and validation for email format and termination dates.

solutionList.title: Schedule Management
solutionList.description: Per-employee work schedules with date/time windows, shift types, and conflict validation (start_time must precede end_time).

solutionList.title: Vacation Workflow
solutionList.description: Vacation requests with PENDING → APPROVED/REJECTED transitions, reviewer tracking, and manager comments.

solutionList.title: Async Infrastructure
solutionList.description: Async SQLAlchemy + optional Upstash Redis cache, FastAPI dependency injection, and use-case containers per bounded context (employees, schedules, vacations).

solutionList.title: Event-Driven Processing
solutionList.description: Kafka consumer on notification.incoming, cinema.user-service.events, and wallet.events — auto-creates and sends notifications with event_id deduplication.

solutionList.title: Multi-Channel Delivery
solutionList.description: Single domain model routed to Email (SMTP), SMS (Twilio), Push, or In-App via SendingService port/adapter.

solutionList.title: MongoDB Document Store
solutionList.description: Async Motor persistence for full notification lifecycle, filters, and pagination by user, type, channel, and attention status.

solutionList.title: Attention Tracking
solutionList.description: Flags important or failed notifications (OPEN → ACKNOWLEDGED → RESOLVED) for operational follow-up.

solutionList.title: Domain-Driven Payment Aggregate
solutionList.description: Payment aggregate root with lifecycle (pending → processing → completed/failed), refund rules, and domain events (PaymentCreated, PaymentCompleted, etc.).

solutionList.title: Event-Driven Communication
solutionList.description: Kafka publishers for payment.events; consumers on payment.incoming, billboard.events, and wallet.events when enabled.

solutionList.title: Multi-Provider Gateway
solutionList.description: PaymentGateway port with Stripe adapter; wallet and catalog payment methods.

solutionList.title: Role-Split REST APIs
solutionList.description: Customer self-service, staff operations (show revenue, refunds), and admin overrides/search under separate route prefixes.

solutionList.title: RESTful Ticket API
solutionList.description: Command/query split routers for buy, cancel, use, search, and quotes at /api/v2/tickets.

solutionList.title: Mongo Billboard Replica
solutionList.description: Read-heavy showtime, theater, and seat data replicated from Kafka billboard.events.

solutionList.title: PostgreSQL Ticket Store
solutionList.description: Authoritative ticket records, transactions, and seat assignments.

solutionList.title: gRPC External Assertions
solutionList.description: Outbound clients to GRPC_BILLBOARD_TARGET and GRPC_PAYMENT_TARGET when configured.

solutionList.title: QR Confirmation Payload
solutionList.description: TicketBuyedResponse includes QR data for door scanning.

solutionList.title: Hexagonal Architecture
solutionList.description: Domain, application, and infrastructure layers with ports/adapters per bounded context (users, auth, profile).

solutionList.title: JWT Session Management
solutionList.description: Stateless access tokens plus Redis-backed refresh tokens for logout and refresh flows.

solutionList.title: Two-Factor Authentication
solutionList.description: TOTP with QR enrollment and step-up login via /auth/2FA/access.

solutionList.title: Event-Driven Lifecycle
solutionList.description: Kafka publisher for user events when KAFKA_ENABLED (signup, activation, etc.).

solutionList.title: Dual Protocol
solutionList.description: REST for clients; gRPC UsersService (GetUserById/Email/Phone) for internal callers.

solutionList.title: Wallet Aggregate
solutionList.description: `Wallet` entity owns balance mutations and transaction creation — add_credit, buy_product, with WalletDomainValidator enforcing currency and amount rules.

solutionList.title: Three API Surfaces
solutionList.description: Staff routes (`/api/v2/wallets`), customer routes (`/api/v2/wallets/users`), and management summary (`/api/v2/wallets/management`).

solutionList.title: Payment Orchestration
solutionList.description: Add-credit and pay use cases coordinate with PaymentInternalService port (gRPC stub today) before persisting wallet state.

solutionList.title: Cloud-Native Deployment
solutionList.description: Docker on EC2, PostgreSQL on RDS, Upstash Redis for cache validation, optional Kafka for wallet.events topic.

decisions.title: FastAPI over Flask/Django
decisions.problem: Need async-capable, high-performance REST with automatic OpenAPI.
decisions.solution: FastAPI with native async, Pydantic validation, and built-in /docs.
decisions.outcome: Low-latency handlers, type-safe DTOs, self-documenting API.
alternatives: Flask with async extensions (less mature typing)

decisions.title: gRPC for Catalog Integration
decisions.problem: Low-latency service-to-service validation of movies and theaters.
decisions.solution: gRPC + protobuf CatalogGateway adapter.
decisions.outcome: Sub-30 ms catalog lookups under normal network conditions.

decisions.title: Business Rules in Domain Entity
decisions.problem: Validation logic duplicated across use cases and API layer.
decisions.solution: Centralize in Showtime.validate_business_logic() and lifecycle methods.
decisions.outcome: Testable, reusable rules independent of HTTP framework.

decisions.title: Embedded gRPC Server
decisions.problem: Peer services need fast catalog validation without HTTP overhead.
decisions.solution: Start grpc.aio.Server in FastAPI lifespan alongside REST.
decisions.outcome: Single deployable unit; sub-30 ms lookups for movie/theater checks.

decisions.title: Multi-Package Domains
decisions.problem: Movies, cinemas, theaters have different rules but share infra.
decisions.solution: app/movies, app/cinema, app/theater packages with shared core.
decisions.outcome: Clear boundaries; independent evolution per domain.

decisions.title: Theater Capacity in Domain
decisions.problem: Invalid capacities must be rejected before DB write.
decisions.solution: CAPACITY_RULES ClassVar on Theater entity with validate_capacity().
decisions.outcome: Testable rules; consistent errors across REST and future gRPC mutations.

decisions.title: CQRS over anemic services
decisions.problem: Read and write shapes differ (search vs create).
decisions.solution: Explicit Command/Query types per use case.
decisions.outcome: Clearer tests; optimized query objects for pagination.
alternatives: Single service class per entity (more coupling)

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Gunicorn workers not ideal for long-lived gRPC server.
decisions.solution: docker-entrypoint serve-grpc runs app.grpc.server standalone.
decisions.outcome: Independent scaling and failure isolation for RPC.
alternatives: Embed gRPC in FastAPI lifespan (catalog pattern)

decisions.title: UUID value objects
decisions.problem: String IDs prone to format errors across services.
decisions.solution: ProductId/ComboId/PromotionId with from_string validation.
decisions.outcome: Consistent parsing in REST and gRPC layers.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over ElastiCache
decisions.problem: Need managed Redis without VPC cluster ops for a portfolio-scale deployment.
decisions.solution: Use Upstash serverless Redis with TLS URL in REDIS_URL; graceful fallback if unavailable.
decisions.outcome: Lower ops overhead; cache optional so API remains functional without Redis.
alternatives: AWS ElastiCache in same VPC as EC2
In-memory cache (rejected — not shared across replicas)

decisions.title: Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Durable workforce data with backups and SSL.
decisions.solution: RDS PostgreSQL with POSTGRES_SSL_MODE=require from EC2.
decisions.outcome: Managed backups, patching, and connection endpoint separate from app host.

decisions.title: EC2 + Docker Compose (prod)
decisions.problem: Simple deployment for a single-region cinema platform demo.
decisions.solution: docker-compose.prod.yml runs app container only; external RDS and Upstash via .env.
decisions.outcome: Portable image; same Dockerfile for local and cloud.

decisions.title: MongoDB over PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Notification documents vary by channel with nested content/recipient.
decisions.solution: Document store with flexible schema and Motor async driver.
decisions.outcome: Fast writes and rich filter queries without heavy joins.

decisions.title: In-process Kafka consumer
decisions.problem: Need event-driven notifications without separate worker deploy initially.
decisions.solution: asyncio tasks in FastAPI lifespan calling kafka-python poll loop.
decisions.outcome: Single deployable unit; consumer stops cleanly on shutdown.

decisions.title: Jinja2 email templates
decisions.problem: Consistent branding across notification types.
decisions.solution: Template map per NotificationType in EmailService.
decisions.outcome: HTML emails with injected template_data from content.data.

decisions.title: PostgreSQL for payments
decisions.problem: ACID transactions, audit trail, and relational reporting.
decisions.solution: Async SQLAlchemy with Alembic migrations on cinema_payment DB.
decisions.outcome: Strong consistency for money movement and admin summaries.

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Isolate long-lived gRPC server from HTTP workers.
decisions.solution: app-grpc service with serve-grpc entrypoint on port 50051.
decisions.outcome: Ready for inbound RPC; servicers not registered yet.
alternatives: Embedded gRPC in same process as REST

decisions.title: Stripe as primary gateway
decisions.problem: Card payments and stored payment methods with PCI scope reduction.
decisions.solution: PaymentGateway port with Stripe adapter and token storage.
decisions.outcome: PaymentIntent flow with webhook handler on staff routes.
alternatives: PayPal / Adyen adapters (catalog supports provider enum)

decisions.title: Mongo for billboard replica
decisions.problem: Ticket reads must not synchronously call billboard on every quote.
decisions.solution: Event-synced Mongo collections from Kafka billboard.events.
decisions.outcome: Sub-50ms reads; eventual consistency acceptable for quotes.
alternatives: Synchronous HTTP to billboard on every request

decisions.title: PostgreSQL for tickets
decisions.problem: ACID seat assignment and ticket lifecycle auditing.
decisions.solution: Relational ticket and seat tables with Alembic migrations.
decisions.outcome: Strong consistency for sold seats and status transitions.
alternatives: Tickets only in Mongo (weaker transactional guarantees)

decisions.title: CQRS routers
decisions.problem: Write operations need stricter limits and different OpenAPI tags.
decisions.solution: Separate command and query APIRouter modules, same prefix.
decisions.outcome: Clear Swagger grouping; independent rate limit policies.

decisions.title: Redis for refresh tokens
decisions.problem: Need revocable sessions with logout-all support.
decisions.solution: Store refresh tokens in Redis; access tokens remain stateless JWT.
decisions.outcome: Fast invalidation without DB round-trips on every API call.

decisions.title: Integer user IDs
decisions.problem: Legacy schema and sequential admin tooling.
decisions.solution: Auto-increment integer PK on users table.
decisions.outcome: Simpler joins in ticket service; differs from UUID-based services.

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Directory lookups should not compete with HTTP worker pool.
decisions.solution: app-grpc with serve-grpc entrypoint on port 50051.
decisions.outcome: Independent scaling; verify app.grpc module path matches sources.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over ElastiCache
decisions.problem: Managed Redis without VPC cluster administration for portfolio deployment.
decisions.solution: Upstash TLS URL in REDIS_URL from EC2 .env.
decisions.outcome: Lower ops overhead; cache optional.

decisions.title: Kafka over RabbitMQ
decisions.problem: Legacy README referenced RabbitMQ; codebase uses kafka-python.
decisions.solution: Optional Kafka publisher with noop fallback when KAFKA_ENABLED=false.
decisions.outcome: Aligns with other cinema platform services; no RabbitMQ dependency.

decisions.title: UUID wallet identifiers
decisions.problem: Distributed user-service integration needs stable external IDs.
decisions.solution: WalletId and UserId value objects wrapping UUID.
decisions.outcome: JWT sub maps directly to user_id for customer routes.

codeExamples.title: Application Lifespan — Startup Checks
codeExamples.description: FastAPI lifespan hook for Postgres validation, Redis init, and graceful shutdown.

files.name: main.py
files.explanation: Validates DB connectivity and initializes cache before serving traffic.
files.content:
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    logger.info("Starting up Billboard Service...")
    await run_postgres_startup_check(engine)
    await init_cache()
    yield
    logger.info("Shutting down Billboard Service...")
    await close_cache()

codeExamples.title: gRPC Catalog Servicer
codeExamples.description: IsMovieActive RPC used by billboard-service for validation.

files.name: catalog_server.py
files.explanation: Opens async session per RPC; returns is_active from domain entity.
files.content:
async def IsMovieActive(self, request, context):
    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session:
        repo = SQLAlchemyMovieRepository(session)
        movie = await repo.find_by_id(request.movie_id)
        return catalog_pb2.IsMovieActiveResponse(
            is_active=bool(movie and movie.is_active)
        )

codeExamples.title: Lifespan — gRPC Co-Startup
codeExamples.description: FastAPI lifespan starts gRPC server alongside REST.

files.name: main.py
files.explanation: gRPC server stopped gracefully on shutdown (5s grace).
files.content:
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    await run_postgres_startup_check(engine)
    grpc_server = create_catalog_grpc_server()
    await grpc_server.start()
    app.state.grpc_server = grpc_server
    yield
    await grpc_server.stop(grace=5)

codeExamples.title: Notification Aggregate — Lifecycle

files.name: models.py
files.explanation: State transitions update status timestamps and clear error fields on success.
files.content:
def mark_as_sent(self, sent_at: Optional[datetime] = None) -> None:
    self.status = NotificationStatus.SENT
    self.sent_at = sent_at or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    self.failed_at = None
    self.error_details = None

def mark_as_failed(self, error_details: str, ...) -> None:
    self.status = NotificationStatus.FAILED
    self.failed_at = failed_at or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    self.error_details = error_details

def mark_attention_open(self) -> None:
    self.is_important = True
    self.attention_status = NotificationAttentionStatus.OPEN

            recipient=command.recipient,

async def send(self, notification: Notification) -> None:
    html_content = self._render_template(
        notification_type=notification.notification_type,
        template_data=notification.content.data or {},
    )
    self._send_email(
        to_email=notification.recipient.email,
        subject=notification.content.subject,
        html_content=html_content,
    )

class StripePaymentGateway(PaymentGateway):
    async def create_payment_intent(self, amount, currency, metadata):
        intent = stripe.PaymentIntent.create(
            amount=int(amount * 100),
            currency=currency.lower(),
            metadata=metadata,
        )
        return intent.id

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage Python 3.13 image with non-root user and built-in healthcheck.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM python:3.13-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt ./
RUN python -m venv /opt/venv && /opt/venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

FROM python:3.13-slim AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /opt/venv /opt/venv
COPY . .
USER app
EXPOSE 8000
HEALTHCHECK CMD python -c "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8000/health')"
CMD ["/app/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh", "serve"]

dockerFiles.description: docker-compose.local.yml — Postgres 16, Redis 7, 2 app replicas, NGINX on 8443.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
  app-1:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
  app-2:
    image: billboard-service:local
  nginx:
    ports: ["8443:443"]

FROM python:3.13-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt ./
RUN python -m venv /opt/venv && /opt/venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

FROM python:3.13-slim AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /opt/venv /opt/venv
COPY . .
USER app
EXPOSE 8000

dockerFiles.description: docker-compose.local.yml — Postgres 16, Redis 7, 3 app replicas, NGINX on 8447.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    ports: ["5436:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports: ["6384:6379"]
  app-1:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
    environment: { RUN_MIGRATIONS_ON_START: "1" }
  app-2:
    image: employee-service:local
  app-3:
    image: employee-service:local
  nginx:
    ports: ["8084:80", "8447:443"]

dockerFiles.description: docker-compose.prod.yml — application container only; RDS + Upstash from .env.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
    env_file: [../.env]
    ports: ["${API_PORT:-8000}:8000"]
    command: ["/app/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh", "serve"]

dockerFiles.description: Referenced in README; Dockerfile/docker-compose not present in repository at time of doc generation — run locally with uvicorn or add compose file.
dockerFiles.content:
# Suggested local run (no committed Dockerfile):
pip install -r requirements.txt
alembic upgrade head  # if migrations added
uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

# Production pattern (from deprecated docs):
FROM python:3.13-slim
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

dockerFiles.description: Multi-replica HTTP + separate gRPC + nginx load balancer.
dockerFiles.content:
# docker/docker-compose.yml (summary)
services:
  db:        postgres:16  → host 5431
  redis:     redis:7      → host 6378
  app-1:     payment-service:local (migrations on start)
  app-2:     payment-service:local
  app-3:     payment-service:local
  app-grpc:  payment-service:local serve-grpc → 50051
  nginx:     load balances app-1..3
  migrate:   profile tools — alembic upgrade

# Local without Docker:
uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

# docker/docker-compose.yml (summary)
name: account-service

  db:     postgres:16  → host 5431 (cinema_account)
  redis:  redis:7      → host 6378
  app-1:  account-service:local (migrations + gunicorn)
  app-2:  account-service:local
  app-3:  account-service:local
  app-grpc: serve-grpc → 50051
  nginx:  ports 80/443
  migrate: profile tools

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage Python 3.13 image; exposes 8000 and 50051; non-root user.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM python:3.13-slim AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN python -m venv /opt/venv && /opt/venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

FROM python:3.13-slim AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /opt/venv /opt/venv
COPY . .
USER app
EXPOSE 8000 50051
HEALTHCHECK CMD python -c "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8000/health')"
CMD ["/app/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh", "serve"]

dockerFiles.description: docker-compose.yml — Postgres 16, Redis 7, 3 REST replicas, gRPC, NGINX.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    ports: ["5431:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports: ["6378:6379"]
  app-1:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
  app-2:
    image: wallet-service:local
  app-3:
    image: wallet-service:local
  app-grpc:
    command: ["/app/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh", "serve-grpc"]
    ports: ["50051:50051"]
  nginx:
    ports: ["80:80", "443:443"]

dockerFiles.description: docker-compose.prod.yml — REST container; optional gRPC profile.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
    env_file: [../.env]
    ports: ["${API_PORT:-8000}:8000"]
  app-grpc:
    profiles: [grpc]
    command: ["/app/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh", "serve-grpc"]
    ports: ["${GRPC_PORT:-50051}:${GRPC_PORT:-50051}"]

name: Company Expenses API
description: REST API for corporate expense reporting: employees submit claims with receipts, managers approve or reject, finance processes reimbursements, and admins configure company settings—with JWT security, Flyway migrations, and Docker deploy.

problemStatement.problemDescription: Companies often track employee expenses in spreadsheets or email threads. Managers lack a single queue for pending approvals, finance cannot tie reimbursements to approved claims, and employees have no audit trail for receipts or rejection reasons.
problemList: Expense status scattered across email and spreadsheets
No role-separated API for employees vs managers vs finance vs admins
Receipt attachments stored ad hoc without linkage to expense records
Managers need paginated filters by status and monthly summaries
No standardized JSON envelope for frontend clients

solution.solutionTitle: A Spring Boot API for the full expense lifecycle

solutionList.title: JWT auth with role-based routes
solutionList.description: Register employees and managers publicly; admins register other admins. OAuth2 resource server validates Bearer tokens with roles claim mapped to Spring authorities.

solutionList.title: Employee expense submission
solutionList.description: Authenticated employees create PENDING expenses, upload multipart attachments, and list their own claims via JWT subject email.

solutionList.title: Manager approval workflow
solutionList.description: Managers and admins approve or reject expenses, soft-delete records, filter by status with sorting, and fetch date-range summaries.

solutionList.title: Finance reimbursements
solutionList.description: MANAGER or FINANCE roles create reimbursements linked to expenses; notifications fire on state changes.

solutionList.title: Admin settings & dashboard
solutionList.description: Admin-only dashboard stats and mutable company settings stored in the database.

solutionList.title: Containerized local and cloud deploy
solutionList.description: Multi-stage Docker image, local compose with PostgreSQL 15, production compose with external DB host from `.env`.

metricsList: ~75 main Java source files + 11 test classes
2 Flyway SQL migrations (schema + demo seed data)
API served on port 8080; Swagger UI at `/swagger-ui.html`
Global Bucket4j interceptor on `/**` (20 token bucket, 10 refill/min)

designPatterns.title: Layered architecture
designPatterns.description: Controllers delegate to services; services use repositories and mappers—classic Spring layering.

designPatterns.title: Result object

designPatterns.title: Response wrapper

designPatterns.title: Repository

designPatterns.title: Interceptor — rate limit

techDecisions.title: Spring Boot monolith
techDecisions.problem: Small team needs one deployable unit with clear modules, not microservice overhead.
techDecisions.solution: Single Gradle project with controller/service/repository packages.
techDecisions.outcome: Faster iteration; Docker one-jar deploy.

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL + Flyway
techDecisions.problem: Relational expense data with FK integrity and audit fields.
techDecisions.solution: PostgreSQL with versioned SQL migrations instead of ddl-auto in prod.
techDecisions.outcome: Predictable schema; demo seed in V2 migration.

techDecisions.title: JWT (OAuth2 resource server)
techDecisions.problem: SPA/mobile clients need stateless auth across instances.
techDecisions.solution: Encode roles in JWT; validate with JwtDecoder per request.
techDecisions.outcome: Works with horizontal scaling; no refresh token flow yet.

techDecisions.title: In-process rate limiting
techDecisions.problem: Basic abuse protection without Redis infrastructure.
techDecisions.solution: Bucket4j bucket in RateLimitInterceptor for all paths.
techDecisions.outcome: Simple for single instance; per-instance counters when scaled out.

codeExamples.title: JWT issuance with role claims
codeExamples.description: JWTService encodes subject (email) and roles list for Spring Security resource server consumption.

files.name: JWTService.java
files.explanation: Tokens include issuer, expiry, subject, and roles claim stripped of ROLE_ prefix in authorities mapping.

codeExamples.title: Path-based security rules
codeExamples.description: SecurityConfig declares public auth/swagger routes and role requirements per API prefix.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: Note FINANCIAL vs Role.FINANCE mismatch for reimbursement routes.
files.content:
.authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/auth/**", "/swagger-ui/**", "/api-docs/**").permitAll()
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/admin/**").hasRole("ADMIN")
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/employees/**").authenticated()
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/manager/**").hasAnyRole("MANAGER", "ADMIN")
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/reimbursements/**").hasAnyRole("MANAGER", "FINANCIAL")
    .anyRequest().authenticated())
.oauth2ResourceServer(oauth2 -> oauth2.jwt(jwt -> jwt
    .decoder(jwtDecoder)
    .jwtAuthenticationConverter(jwtAuthenticationConverter())));

codeExamples.description: ResponseWrapper factory methods keep HTTP payloads consistent for clients.

dockerFiles.description: Local development: Spring Boot app + PostgreSQL 15 with healthcheck and named volume.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    env_file:
      - ../.env
    environment:
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "5432"
  db:
    image: postgres:15
    ports:
      - "5431:5432"
volumes:
  postgres_data:

dockerFiles.description: Production profile: app container only; DB_HOST points to external Postgres.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    env_file:
      - ../.env
    restart: unless-stopped

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage: Gradle 8.5 JDK 17 build (skip tests), Temurin 17 JRE runs app.jar.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM gradle:8.5-jdk17 AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN gradle build -x test
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy
COPY --from=build /app/build/libs/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

name: Cooking Recipes API
description: REST API for a cooking-recipes platform: user accounts with JWT sessions, recipe CRUD, advanced search, favorites, reviews, and view analytics—built with Clean Architecture and deployable on AWS.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Scattered recipe data and weak discovery
problemStatement.problemDescription: Home cooks and food bloggers need one backend to publish structured recipes (ingredients, steps, nutrition), let users search and filter by cuisine or diet, save favorites, and leave ratings—without coupling the UI to SQLite files or ad-hoc JSON.
problemList: Recipe content lives in spreadsheets or static JSON with no auth or ownership
Search cannot combine filters (cuisine, meal type, cooking time, rating) efficiently
No session model for multi-device login and secure logout
Favorites and reviews duplicated across clients with no single source of truth
No production path from local SQLite to managed PostgreSQL on AWS

solution.solutionTitle: A FastAPI backend with DDD modules

solutionList.title: Auth module with JWT + Redis sessions
solutionList.description: Signup/login, refresh tokens stored in Redis, logout with optional revoke-all-devices; bcrypt password hashing.

solutionList.title: Recipe module with use cases
solutionList.description: Create/update/soft-delete recipes, specification-based search, featured list, view counter, favorites toggle, and reviews.

solutionList.title: Clean Architecture boundaries
solutionList.description: Domain entities and repository interfaces; application use cases; SQLAlchemy and Redis in infrastructure; FastAPI controllers in presentation.

solutionList.title: Operational readiness
solutionList.description: Docker image, health checks, Alembic migrations via start.sh, rate limiting, CORS for Angular frontend on :4200.

solutionList.title: AWS-ready deploy
solutionList.description: Documented path to ECS + RDS + ElastiCache; local docker-compose bundles Redis; production uses external managed services.

metricsList: 2 bounded contexts: auth, recipe (~94 Python modules under app/)
15+ recipe endpoints (CRUD, search, favorites, reviews, featured)
5 auth endpoints live + 2 session endpoints stubbed (hidden from OpenAPI)

mediaGallery.title: Cooking Recipes API — product views

mediaItems.description: Presentation → Application → Domain → Infrastructure

layers.name: Presentation (clients)
layers.description: Angular SPA and future mobile clients consuming JSON over HTTPS.

layers.name: Edge & API gateway
layers.description: TLS termination and routing to FastAPI on ECS.

layers.name: Application (FastAPI)

components: auth — controllers, use cases, JWT middleware handlers
responsibilities: Map HTTP to use case execute()

layers.name: Domain
layers.description: Entities, value objects, repository interfaces, domain exceptions.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: SQLAlchemy async repositories, Redis session store, JWT and bcrypt services.

designPatterns.title: Clean Architecture
designPatterns.description: Dependencies point inward: presentation → application → domain; infrastructure implements domain ports.

designPatterns.title: Use case (application service)
designPatterns.description: One class per operation (CreateRecipeUseCase, SearchRecipesUseCase) orchestrates domain and repositories.

designPatterns.title: Specification
designPatterns.description: Composable RecipeByNameSpecification, AndSpecification, etc. translated to SQL via specification_builder.

designPatterns.title: Repository
designPatterns.description: IRecipeRepository, IUserRepository abstract persistence; SQLAlchemy implementations in infrastructure.

designPatterns.title: Dependency injection
designPatterns.description: FastAPI Depends() wires use cases and repositories per request in presentation/dependencies.py.

designPatterns.title: Soft delete
designPatterns.description: Recipes marked deleted remain in DB; restore endpoint reverses flag; search excludes deleted by default.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Horizontally scale ECS tasks
scalabilityStrategies.description: Stateless API containers behind ALB; session state in ElastiCache Redis, not in process memory.

scalabilityStrategies.title: RDS PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: Move from SQLite to RDS with connection pool (DB_POOL_SIZE) for production traffic.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Read-heavy recipe search
scalabilityStrategies.description: Specification-built queries with pagination; add DB indexes on name, author_id, rating per DATABASE_SCHEMA.md.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Optional CDN for static media
scalabilityStrategies.description: Profile pictures and recipe images can later use S3 + CloudFront (profile_picture_url field ready).

securityStrategies.title: JWT access + refresh
securityStrategies.description: Short-lived access token (30 min); refresh validated against Redis session store.

securityStrategies.title: bcrypt passwords
securityStrategies.description: BcryptPasswordHasher in infrastructure; strong password rules on signup DTO.

securityStrategies.title: Author-only mutations
securityStrategies.description: Update/delete recipe and reviews require authenticated author checks in use cases.

securityStrategies.title: Rate limiting
securityStrategies.description: Per-IP sliding window: strict (auth), sensitive (writes), public (browse), generous (profile).

securityStrategies.title: Secrets in AWS
securityStrategies.description: JWT_SECRET_KEY from Secrets Manager in ECS task definition—not baked in Docker image for prod.

cacheStrategies.name: Redis session store
cacheStrategies.description: Refresh tokens and session metadata keyed with REDIS_SESSION_PREFIX

cacheStrategies.name: In-process rate limit counters
cacheStrategies.description: RateLimitManager defaultdict per endpoint:IP in app memory
cacheStrategies.coverage: All routed endpoints via global Depends

cacheStrategies.name: Future: Redis rate limits
cacheStrategies.description: Documented in DEPLOYMENT.md for multi-task ECS fairness
cacheStrategies.coverage: Placeholder for shared limiter across replicas

architectureFeatures.title: Auto OpenAPI
architectureFeatures.description: FastAPI generates /docs and /redoc from Pydantic models and route metadata.

architectureFeatures.title: Global exception handling
architectureFeatures.description: GlobalExceptionHandler maps domain errors to consistent HTTP responses.

architectureFeatures.title: Lifespan hooks
architectureFeatures.description: init_db, initialize_redis on startup; graceful close on shutdown.

architectureFeatures.title: View analytics
architectureFeatures.description: GET /recipes/{id} increments view count via IncrementViewCountUseCase.

requestFlow.title: HTTP request
requestFlow.description: Client calls /api/v1/... with optional Authorization Bearer token.

requestFlow.title: Rate limit & auth
requestFlow.description: Global rate_limiter_dependency runs; get_current_user decodes JWT for protected routes.

requestFlow.title: Use case
requestFlow.description: Controller invokes use case execute() with DTOs and domain IDs.

requestFlow.title: Repository / spec
requestFlow.description: Infrastructure runs SQLAlchemy query from specification or loads entity by ID.

requestFlow.title: JSON response
requestFlow.description: Pydantic response_model serializes result to client.

eventFlow.title: Recipe viewed
eventFlow.description: GET /recipes/{id} triggers IncrementViewCountUseCase before returning payload.

eventFlow.title: Review submitted
eventFlow.description: CreateReviewUseCase updates aggregate rating on recipe.

eventFlow.title: Session created
eventFlow.description: Login/signup stores refresh token metadata in Redis via RedisUserSessionRepository.

eventFlow.title: Logout
eventFlow.description: Logout use case deletes session key(s) from Redis; optional logout_all revokes all devices.

techDecisions.title: FastAPI over Django
techDecisions.problem: API-only backend with async I/O and automatic OpenAPI for a separate Angular frontend.
techDecisions.solution: FastAPI + Pydantic for validation and docs; async SQLAlchemy sessions.
techDecisions.outcome: Lightweight, typed DTOs, fast iteration for recipe search endpoints.

techDecisions.title: Clean Architecture + DDD
techDecisions.problem: Recipe domain complexity (search specs, reviews, soft delete) must stay testable.
techDecisions.solution: Separate domain/application/infrastructure per module; use cases as single entry points.
techDecisions.outcome: Unit tests on use cases and specifications without HTTP.

techDecisions.title: SQLite local, PostgreSQL AWS
techDecisions.problem: Simple local dev; production needs managed RDS.
techDecisions.solution: DATABASE_URL drives engine; docker-compose mounts cooking_app.db for quick demos.
techDecisions.outcome: Alembic migrations via start.sh; switch env for ECS deploy.

techDecisions.title: Redis for sessions only
techDecisions.problem: Refresh token revocation and multi-device logout require server-side state.
techDecisions.solution: ElastiCache Redis in AWS; docker-compose redis service on port 6378 locally.
techDecisions.outcome: Secure logout; refresh fails with 404 if session removed.

codeExamples.title: Application lifespan (DB + Redis)
codeExamples.description: main.py wires async startup/shutdown for database and Redis used by auth sessions.

files.name: main.py
files.explanation: ECS tasks rely on successful Redis connect before accepting traffic if health checks include dependency probes.
files.content:
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    await init_db()
    await initialize_redis()
    yield
    await close_redis()
    await close_db()

app = FastAPI(
    title="Cooking Recipes API",
    version="1.0.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

dockerFiles.description: Local stack: backend + Redis; SQLite file bind-mount; healthcheck on /health.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  backend:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
      - "8443:8443"
    volumes:
      - ./cooking_app.db:/app/cooking_app.db
      - ./logs:/app/logs
    environment:
      - DEBUG=false
    restart: unless-stopped
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6378:6379"

dockerFiles.description: python:3.11-slim; installs gcc+curl; copies app, optional cert.pem/key.pem/.env; HEALTHCHECK curl /health; CMD ./start.sh.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gcc curl
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
EXPOSE 8080 8443
HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health
CMD ["./start.sh"]

dockerFiles.description: Runs alembic upgrade head if needed, then exec python main.py (Uvicorn).

# Check alembic_version table; migrate if missing
alembic upgrade head
exec python main.py
