name: Drugstore Platform
description: Multi-service drugstore e-commerce platform built with Java 23 and Spring Boot. Eleven bounded contexts (auth, users, catalog, cart, orders, payments, inventory, stores, addresses, employees, notifications) communicate via REST, gRPC, and message buses — backed by PostgreSQL, Redis, and observability tooling.

problemStatement.problemDescription:
Combined problem descriptions from all Drugstore Platform services.

# address-service
E-commerce platforms require robust address management supporting multiple countries with different postal code formats, role-based access (customers vs employees), default address handling, and limits per user type. Without a dedicated service, address logic couples to user management and lacks validation.

# auth-service
In a microservices e-commerce platform, authentication must be centralized to avoid duplication across services. Requirements include JWT token management, refresh token rotation, two-factor authentication, OAuth2 social login, password reset flows, and publishing auth events to other services via Kafka.

# cart-service
In a microservices e-commerce platform, shopping cart functionality must be isolated from other services while providing real-time cart management, product availability checks, save-for-later (afterwards) feature, and efficient inter-service communication via gRPC.

# employee-service
A drugstore pharmacy needs comprehensive employee management including multiple roles (pharmacists, technicians, managers), employment types (full-time, part-time, contractor), status tracking (active, on-leave, suspended, terminated), certifications tracking, compensation management, and workday schedule storage.

# inventory-service
A drugstore pharmacy needs comprehensive inventory management including batch tracking (lot numbers, expiration dates), stock reservations for order processing, inventory movements (adjustments, transfers between warehouses), low-stock alerts, and event publishing for inventory changes.

# order-service
A drugstore platform needs a robust order management system that can handle multiple delivery methods (store pickup, express delivery, standard delivery), complex state transitions, item validation, and integration with external services like user management and address validation. The system must enforce business rules, handle events for asynchronous processing, and provide comprehensive API for both customers and administrators.

# payment-service
A drugstore platform needs a reliable payment processing system that can handle multiple payment methods, integrate with external payment gateways (Stripe), manage the complete payment lifecycle including refunds, and automatically generate sales records. The system must handle webhooks from payment providers, maintain idempotency, and provide traceability between payments and orders.

# product-service
Drugstore platform needs a single source of truth for product data with validation, fast reads, and cross-service event propagation.

# store-service
Product and order services need a single source of truth for store identity, address, schedule, geolocation, and operational status across channels.

# user-service
Order, cart, and store services need a single source of truth for user records, credentials validation, lifecycle (activate/ban/delete), and customer profile data without duplicating persistence rules.
problemList: No centralized address validation for multiple countries (US, MX, CA, ES, UK postal codes)
Lack of role-based address limits (customers can have multiple, employees limited to one)
No default address management mechanism
Missing rate limiting and security for address operations
Address data scattered across services without proper abstraction
No centralized auth service across microservices
JWT access + refresh token management needed
Two-factor authentication (TOTP) for enhanced security
OAuth2 social login (Google, GitHub, etc.)
Password reset flow with email tokens
Kafka event publishing for user lifecycle events
gRPC communication with user-service for user data
Need isolated cart aggregate with business logic encapsulation
Support for cart items with quantity management
Save-for-later (afterwards) feature for items not ready for checkout
gRPC endpoints for order-service to access carts during checkout
Kafka event consumption for product updates (price changes, availability)
Redis caching for frequently accessed cart data
Pagination and search capabilities for admin management
Need to manage diverse employee roles with specific permissions
Track employment status changes (active → suspended → terminated)
Store complex workday schedules per employee
Manage employee certifications (expirations, renewals)
Track compensation (hourly rate, weekly hours)
Soft delete support for employee termination
Audit fields for created/updated/deleted tracking
Track inventory per product with total/available/reserved quantities
Manage inventory batches with lot numbers, manufacturing/expiration dates
Support stock reservations for pending orders (prevent overselling)
Track inventory movements (adjustments, transfers) for audit trail
Generate low-stock alerts based on reorder levels
Publish inventory events to message queue for other services
Warehouse location tracking for multi-location support
Managing complex order state transitions with validation rules
Supporting multiple delivery methods with different business logic
Handling order items with prescription drug validation
Integrating with external user and address services
Providing role-based API access for customers, employees, and admins
Implementing event-driven architecture for order lifecycle events
Caching frequently accessed data for performance
Managing complex payment state transitions (PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED → REFUNDED)
Integrating with external payment gateway (Stripe) for PaymentIntents and refunds
Handling asynchronous webhook events from Stripe with signature verification
Managing two aggregate roots (Payment and Sale) with different lifecycles
Supporting multiple payment methods (Credit Card, Debit, Cash, Bank Transfer, Digital Wallet)
Processing full and partial refunds with gateway synchronization
Maintaining idempotency for webhook event processing
Providing traceability between Payment, Sale, and Order entities
Inconsistent product data across services without a canonical owner.
Need controlled write access with JWT roles (ADMIN/MANAGER).
Read-heavy catalog lookups need caching to reduce DB load.
Other domains require product change events in near real-time.
Store data must stay consistent with address and business rules (status transitions, soft delete).
Read-heavy discovery flows need low latency under concurrent traffic.
Operators need secured mutation APIs while storefronts may read catalog data.
REST API for authenticated reads and manager/admin mutations
Kafka consumption for user.created / user.updated / user.deleted side effects
Inter-service checks (uniqueness, credentials) intended via gRPC (see architecture notes)
Redis-backed caching and rate limiting hooks (shared-kernel)

solutionList.title: Multi-Country Postal Code Validation
solutionList.description: Factory pattern implementation supporting country-specific postal code validators (US ZIP, MX CP, CA Postal Code, ES Código Postal, UK Postcode) with regex-based format verification

solutionList.title: Role-Based Address Management
solutionList.description: Distinguishes between CUSTOMER (max 5 addresses) and EMPLOYEE (max 1 address) with configurable limits via AddressServiceProperties

solutionList.title: Default Address Handling
solutionList.description: Automatic default address assignment for first address, with ability to set/unset defaults per user with database-level reset

solutionList.title: Dual Controller Architecture
solutionList.description: Separate UserAddressController (for end-users) and AddressAdminController (for admins) with appropriate permission checks and audit logging

solutionList.title: Redis-Backed Rate Limiting
solutionList.description: Configurable rate limiting profiles (STANDARD, SENSITIVE) using Redis with token bucket algorithm via AOP aspect

solutionList.title: DDD Orchestrator Pattern
solutionList.description: UseCasesOrquestrator coordinates all authentication use cases following Domain-Driven Design principles

solutionList.title: JWT Token Management
solutionList.description: TokenFactory creates ACCESS (short-lived) and REFRESH (long-lived) JWT tokens with JJWT library

solutionList.title: Multi-Channel Event Publishing
solutionList.description: UserEventProducer publishes to Kafka topics: user.created, user.updated, user.deleted, auth.password-changed, auth.account-activated, auth.two-factor-enabled/disabled

solutionList.title: Redis Session Management
solutionList.description: RedisSessionRepository manages refresh token sessions with blacklisting support

solutionList.title: gRPC User Service Client
solutionList.description: UserServiceGrpcClient communicates with user-service for user CRUD operations via Protobuf

solutionList.title: Cart Aggregate Root
solutionList.description: Cart.java is the aggregate root with items and afterwardsItems, encapsulating all business logic for add/update/remove/clear operations

solutionList.title: gRPC Service Interface
solutionList.description: CartGrpcService exposes GetUserCart and ClearCart endpoints for order-service integration during checkout flow

solutionList.title: Afterwards (Save-for-Later)
solutionList.description: Separate list for items saved for later, with move-to-afterwards and restore-from-afterwards operations

solutionList.title: Kafka Product Events
solutionList.description: ProductEventConsumer listens to product-events topic and updates cart items via ProductEventHandler

solutionList.title: Value Objects
solutionList.description: CartId, CustomerId, ProductId, Quantity, ItemPrice as strongly-typed value objects with validation

solutionList.title: Redis Caching
solutionList.description: Spring Cache with Redis for cart lookups, configured via RedisCacheConfig

solutionList.title: Rich JPA Entity Model
solutionList.description: EmployeeEntity with embedded objects (ContactInfo, Address), certifications list, workday schedule as JSONB, and comprehensive enums

solutionList.title: Command/Query Separation
solutionList.description: EmployeeCommandController (write) and EmployeeQueryController (read) with dedicated use cases

solutionList.title: Rate Limiting
solutionList.description: Uses @RateLimit annotations from libs-kernel with STANDARD, SENSITIVE, PUBLIC profiles

solutionList.title: Soft Delete Pattern
solutionList.description: deletedAt timestamp for soft deletes, with restore functionality

solutionList.title: Audit Trail
solutionList.description: createdAt, updatedAt, createdBy, lastModifiedBy fields with @PrePersist/@PreUpdate

solutionList.title: Inventory Entity with Batch Tracking
solutionList.description: InventoryEntity tracks total/available/reserved quantities, batches with lot numbers and expiration dates

solutionList.title: Stock Reservation System
solutionList.description: Reservation system prevents overselling during order processing with automatic release on timeout

solutionList.title: Inventory Movements Tracking
solutionList.description: All stock adjustments and transfers tracked via InventoryMovementEntity with audit trail

solutionList.title: Low-Stock Alerts
solutionList.description: InventoryAlertEntity generates alerts when stock falls below reorder level

solutionList.title: RabbitMQ Messaging
solutionList.description: Publishes inventory events (created, updated, low-stock) to RabbitMQ for other services

solutionList.title: Redis Caching
solutionList.description: Spring Cache with Redis for frequently accessed inventory data

solutionList.title: Integration testing (profile test)
solutionList.description: H2 in-memory, MockMvc REST tests, real JWT headers (JwtAuthenticationFilter); see docs/project/generated/ProjectFeature.md

solutionList.title: Hexagonal Architecture Implementation
solutionList.description: Ports and adapters pattern separates domain logic from infrastructure concerns, allowing easy testing and technology swaps

solutionList.title: Rich Domain Model
solutionList.description: Order aggregate root encapsulates business rules, state transitions, and validation logic with methods like confirm(), startPreparing(), ship(), complete(), cancel(), and returnOrder()

solutionList.title: Multiple Delivery Methods
solutionList.description: Supports STORE_PICKUP, EXPRESS_DELIVERY, and STANDARD_DELIVERY with method-specific logic for address validation and pickup info

solutionList.title: Event-Driven Domain Events
solutionList.description: Publishes OrderCreatedEvent and OrderStatusChangedEvent for asynchronous processing and system integration

solutionList.title: Role-Based API Controllers
solutionList.description: Separate controllers for admin operations (SaleOrderController, SaleOrderStatusController) and customer operations (UserOrderController)

solutionList.title: Caching Layer with Redis
solutionList.description: Redis caching for frequently accessed order data with decorator pattern (CachingUserServiceDecorator)

solutionList.title: Comprehensive Validation
solutionList.description: Bean Validation annotations, custom domain validation, and duplicate product detection in orders

solutionList.title: Dual Aggregate Roots
solutionList.description: Payment aggregate manages payment lifecycle; Sale aggregate is auto-generated from completed payments representing confirmed business transactions

solutionList.title: Stripe Integration (Planned)
solutionList.description: StripeGatewayAdapter implements StripeGatewayPort for PaymentIntent creation, charge processing, and refund operations

solutionList.title: Webhook Handling
solutionList.description: Dedicated StripeWebhookController with signature verification, event parsing, and idempotent processing

solutionList.title: State Machine Implementation
solutionList.description: PaymentStatus (6 states) and SaleStatus (4 states) enums with business rule validation for transitions

solutionList.title: Domain Events
solutionList.description: PaymentCompletedEvent and PaymentFailedEvent enable loose coupling and trigger Sale creation

solutionList.title: Spring Event Publishing
solutionList.description: SpringEventPublisherAdapter implements PaymentEventPublisher port for in-process domain event publishing

solutionList.title: Value Objects
solutionList.description: Strongly-typed value objects (PaymentID, OrderID, CustomerID, Money, PaymentGatewayRef, RefundInfo) for type safety

solutionList.title: Catalog API
solutionList.description: REST endpoints for CRUD, search, category listing, and restore of soft-deleted products.

solutionList.title: Validation + Security
solutionList.description: Domain value objects/validators plus JWT-based role authorization and request rate limits.

solutionList.title: Caching + Events
solutionList.description: Redis-backed cache decorators for query use cases and Kafka publishing for product lifecycle events.

solutionList.title: Domain-driven core + adapters
solutionList.description: Commands and queries go through use cases; JPA implements persistence; REST exposes `/api/v2/stores` with OpenAPI.

solutionList.title: Redis-backed caching + rate limits
solutionList.description: Query results cached in named regions; method-level rate limits via shared-kernel profiles (global + per-endpoint).

solutionList.title: Docker-first operations
solutionList.description: Compose brings Postgres, Redis, observability stack, and Nginx TLS in front of scalable store-service replicas.

solutionList.title: Command / query separation
solutionList.description: Write paths use CommandBus; reads use QueryBus over domain ports and JPA adapters.

solutionList.title: JWT stateless API
solutionList.description: libs_kernel JwtAuthenticationFilter; manager routes require ADMIN or MANAGER.

solutionList.title: Docker-first local stack
solutionList.description: Compose: PostgreSQL, Redis, optional external Kafka, Nginx TLS edge, Prometheus, Loki, Grafana.

layers.name: Controllers (Inbound)
layers.description: REST API layer handling HTTP requests with JWT authentication, rate limiting, and role-based access control
components: UserAddressController - Endpoints for users to manage their own addresses
AddressAdminController - Administrative endpoints for managing all addresses

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Business logic orchestration with validation, mapping, and transaction management
components: AddressService - Core business logic for address CRUD operations
responsibilities: Address limit validation per user type

layers.name: Validation Layer
layers.description: Country-specific postal code validation using Factory pattern

layers.name: Data Access Layer
layers.description: JPA-based persistence with PostgreSQL and Flyway migrations
components: AddressEntity - JPA entity with UUID ID, user_id, address fields

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Cross-cutting concerns: security, CORS, rate limiting, OpenAPI, audit logging
components: SecurityConfig - JWT filter chain, role-based authorization
RateLimitAspect - AOP aspect for Redis rate limiting

layers.name: Controllers (Inbound Adapters)
layers.description: REST API layer handling HTTP requests with rate limiting and input validation
components: LoginController - Login, 2FA login, refresh session endpoints
LogoutController - Logout single session or all sessions

layers.name: Application Layer (Use Cases)
layers.description: DDD Application Services coordinating domain logic and orchestrating use cases
components: RegisterUseCase - User registration with role assignment
LogoutUseCases - Single and all sessions logout

layers.name: Domain Layer
layers.description: Core business logic with entities, value objects, and domain events
components: User.java - Core user entity with roles
Value Objects: Email, Password, UserId, UserRole, Token, SessionId, OAuth2Provider, ExternalIDs, PhoneNumber
Domain Events: UserRegisteredEvent, UserLoginEvent, PasswordChangedEvent, AccountActivatedEvent, TwoFactorEnabled/DisabledEvent

layers.name: Output Adapters (Infrastructure)
layers.description: Adapters implementing outbound ports: persistence, messaging, external services
components: TokenManager - JWT and numeric token management (implements TokenService)
TokenFactory - Creates ACCESS, REFRESH, ACTIVATION, TWO_FA tokens
RedisSessionRepository - Refresh token session storage in Redis
UserEventProducer - Kafka event publishing (7+ event types)

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Cross-cutting concerns: security, rate limiting, Kafka, gRPC, CORS
components: SecurityConfig - JWT filter chain, OAuth2, session management

designPatterns.title: Factory Pattern
designPatterns.description: PostalCodeValidatorFactory creates country-specific validators based on ISO country code

designPatterns.title: Strategy Pattern
designPatterns.description: Each country has its own PostalCodeValidator implementation with isValid() and getFormatDescription() methods

designPatterns.title: DTO Pattern
designPatterns.description: Separate DTOs (Address, AddressRequest, AddressSummary) for API requests/responses with Jakarta Validation

designPatterns.title: Repository Pattern
designPatterns.description: AddressRepository abstracts data access using Spring Data JPA with custom query methods

designPatterns.title: Builder Pattern
designPatterns.description: AddressEntity uses @Builder (Lombok) for fluent object creation with optional fields

designPatterns.title: Orchestrator Pattern
designPatterns.description: UseCasesOrquestrator coordinates all authentication use cases, implementing 4 port interfaces (AuthUseCases, LogoutUseCases, PasswordUseCases, RegisterUseCases, TwoFaConfigUseCases)

designPatterns.title: Command Pattern
designPatterns.description: Each action has a Command object (LoginCommand, SignupCommand, ChangePasswordCommand) for clear intent and validation

designPatterns.title: Value Object Pattern
designPatterns.description: Domain values wrapped in objects: Email, Password (BCrypt hashed), UserId, UserRole (enum), Token, SessionId, PhoneNumber, OAuth2Provider, ExternalIDs

designPatterns.title: Factory Pattern
designPatterns.description: TokenFactory creates different token types (ACCESS, REFRESH JWTs; ACTIVATION, TWO_FA numeric) based on TokenType enum

designPatterns.title: Domain Events
designPatterns.description: Events published to Kafka: UserRegisteredEvent, UserCreatedEvent, UserUpdatedEvent, UserDeletedEvent, UserLoginEvent, PasswordChangedEvent, AccountActivatedEvent, TwoFactorEnabled/DisabledEvent

designPatterns.title: Adapter Pattern
designPatterns.description: Output adapters implement port interfaces: TokenManager (TokenService), UserEventProducer (UserEventPublisher), UserServiceGrpcClient (UserServiceClient), RedisSessionRepository (SessionRepository)

designPatterns.title: Aggregate Root Pattern
designPatterns.description: Cart is the aggregate root encapsulating items and afterwardsItems with business logic for add/update/remove/clear operations

designPatterns.title: Value Object Pattern
designPatterns.description: Strongly-typed values: CartId, CustomerId, ProductId, Quantity (with validation), ItemPrice (BigDecimal wrapper), CartTimeStamps

designPatterns.title: Command Pattern
designPatterns.description: Command objects: CreateCartCommand, UpdateCartCommand, ClearCartCommand, CreateAfterwardsCommand, RemoveAfterwardsCommand

designPatterns.title: Repository Pattern
designPatterns.description: CartRepository port interface with CartJpaRepository and CartRepositoryImpl using Specification for search

designPatterns.title: Ports & Adapters
designPatterns.description: CartCommandUseCase/CartQueryUseCase ports with CartCommandUseCaseImpl/CartQueryUseCaseImpl adapters

designPatterns.title: Factory Pattern
designPatterns.description: CartId.generate() for UUID generation, Cart.create() and Cart.reconstruct() factory methods

designPatterns.title: Command Query Separation
designPatterns.description: Separate command and query controllers/use-cases improve clarity and scaling options

designPatterns.title: CQS Pattern
designPatterns.description: Separate command (write) and query (read) operations with dedicated use cases and request objects

designPatterns.title: Value Object Pattern
designPatterns.description: InventoryId, BatchId, MovementId, ReservationId, AlertId as strongly-typed value objects

designPatterns.title: Repository Pattern
designPatterns.description: JPA repositories for Inventory, Batch, Movement, Reservation, Alert entities

designPatterns.title: Entity Pattern with Auditing
designPatterns.description: JPA entities with createdAt, updatedAt fields using @PrePersist/@PreUpdate

designPatterns.title: Reservation Pattern
designPatterns.description: StockReservation entity prevents overselling by reserving stock during order processing

designPatterns.title: Movement Tracking Pattern
designPatterns.description: InventoryMovementEntity tracks all stock adjustments and transfers for audit trail

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal Architecture (Ports and Adapters)
designPatterns.description: Domain-centric architecture with ports (interfaces) defining dependencies and adapters implementing them. Separates business logic from infrastructure concerns.

designPatterns.title: Aggregate Root (DDD)
designPatterns.description: Order serves as Aggregate Root, encapsulating OrderItems and enforcing invariants. All modifications go through the Order entity.

designPatterns.title: Decorator Pattern
designPatterns.description: Used for cross-cutting concerns: CachingUserServiceDecorator and LoggingUserServiceDecorator wrap UserServiceImpl to add caching and logging behavior.

designPatterns.title: Facade Pattern
designPatterns.description: OrderApplicationFacade provides a unified interface combining OrderCommandService and OrderQueryService for simplified client access.

designPatterns.title: Builder Pattern
designPatterns.description: Order entity uses Lombok's @Builder for fluent creation. CreateOrderRequest uses records with factory methods toCommand().

designPatterns.title: Strategy Pattern (Delivery Methods)
designPatterns.description: Different delivery methods (STORE_PICKUP, EXPRESS_DELIVERY, STANDARD_DELIVERY) have method-specific validation and behavior in Order entity.

designPatterns.title: Specification Pattern
designPatterns.description: OrderSpecifications provides reusable query criteria for searching orders with dynamic filters (status, date range, user, etc.).

designPatterns.title: State Pattern (Order Status)
designPatterns.description: OrderStatus enum implements state machine with canTransitionTo() method defining valid state transitions between 9 order states.

designPatterns.title: Mapper Pattern
designPatterns.description: Separate mappers handle object mapping: OrderResponseMapper, OrderDetailResponseMapper, OrderJpaMapper, AddressResponseMapper, UserResponseMapper.

designPatterns.title: Dual Aggregate Roots (DDD)
designPatterns.description: Payment and Sale are separate aggregate roots. Sale is auto-generated from completed Payment, representing confirmed business transactions.

designPatterns.title: Factory Method
designPatterns.description: Payment.create() and Sale.fromPayment() factory methods encapsulate object creation with validation.

designPatterns.title: State Machine (Enum-based)
designPatterns.description: PaymentStatus (6 states) and SaleStatus (4 states) enums define valid state transitions with business rule validation.

designPatterns.title: Port-Adapter Pattern
designPatterns.description: StripeGatewayPort (port) and StripeGatewayAdapter (adapter) separate domain from infrastructure. Easy to swap implementations.

designPatterns.title: Domain Events
designPatterns.description: PaymentCompletedEvent and PaymentFailedEvent enable loose coupling. SpringEventPublisherAdapter for in-process events.

designPatterns.title: Value Object
designPatterns.description: Strongly-typed value objects (PaymentID, OrderID, CustomerID, Money, PaymentGatewayRef) provide type safety.

designPatterns.title: Reconstruct Pattern
designPatterns.description: Payment.reconstruct() and Sale.reconstruct() factory methods for rebuilding objects from persistence (JPA entities).

designPatterns.title: Domain Validation
designPatterns.description: DomainValidation utility class for validating domain invariants (requireNonNull, requireNonBlank, requirePositive).

designPatterns.title: Ports and Adapters (Hexagonal)
designPatterns.description: Core depends on ports while adapters implement infrastructure concerns.

designPatterns.title: Decorator for Caching
designPatterns.description: Cache wrappers augment command/query use-cases without changing controller contracts.

designPatterns.title: Soft Delete
designPatterns.description: Entity uses deleted_at and restore operation to preserve historical records.

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal / Ports & Adapters
designPatterns.description: Inbound REST and outbound JPA/Kafka-ish port implement application interfaces; keeps domain isolated.

designPatterns.title: Transactional script + domain model
designPatterns.description: Use cases load aggregates, mutate domain methods, persist; aligns with pragmatic DDD.

designPatterns.title: Ports and adapters (hexagonal)
designPatterns.description: Input REST + output JPA/gRPC/Kafka implement core ports (UserRepository, buses).

designPatterns.title: CQRS-style buses
designPatterns.description: Explicit CommandBus vs QueryBus instead of god-service injection.

architectureFeatures.title: Multi-Country Support
architectureFeatures.description: Supports postal code validation for US, MX, CA, ES, UK with extensible factory pattern

architectureFeatures.title: Dual Controller Architecture
architectureFeatures.description: Separate controllers for users (own addresses) and admins (all addresses) with proper authorization

architectureFeatures.title: Soft Delete
architectureFeatures.description: Addresses are marked inactive rather than deleted, preserving referential integrity

architectureFeatures.title: Audit Logging
architectureFeatures.description: SLF4J logging for all create/update/delete operations with user and address context

architectureFeatures.title: DDD Bounded Context
architectureFeatures.description: Authentication as a separate bounded context with clear domain model and ports/adapters architecture

architectureFeatures.title: Multi-Role Support
architectureFeatures.description: Supports CUSTOMER, EMPLOYEE, and ADMIN roles with role-specific registration endpoints

architectureFeatures.title: Token Type Flexibility
architectureFeatures.description: Four token types: ACCESS (JWT), REFRESH (JWT), ACTIVATION (numeric), TWO_FA (numeric) via TokenFactory

architectureFeatures.title: Event-Driven Architecture
architectureFeatures.description: Publishes 8+ event types to Kafka for asynchronous inter-service communication

architectureFeatures.title: Integration test stack
architectureFeatures.description: AuthEndpointsIntegrationTest with Spring Boot, Testcontainers Redis/Kafka, profiles integration-test+test, and InMemoryUserGrpcServer for full HTTP flows without mocking core adapters

architectureFeatures.title: DDD Aggregate Root
architectureFeatures.description: Cart aggregate with encapsulated business logic for item management (max 100 unique items)

architectureFeatures.title: Afterwards (Save-for-Later)
architectureFeatures.description: Separate list for items saved for later with move-to and restore-from operations

architectureFeatures.title: gRPC Service Interface
architectureFeatures.description: Exposes GetUserCart and ClearCart for order-service integration during checkout

architectureFeatures.title: Kafka Event Consumption
architectureFeatures.description: Listens to product-events topic to update cart items on price/availability changes

architectureFeatures.title: Domain Events
architectureFeatures.description: CartPurchasedEvent defined but PLACEHOLDER: not published to Kafka yet

architectureFeatures.title: Nginx Front Door
architectureFeatures.description: Single ingress for TLS and load balancing

architectureFeatures.title: Command/Query Split
architectureFeatures.description: Separated controller responsibilities for write/read paths

architectureFeatures.title: Batch Tracking with Expiration
architectureFeatures.description: InventoryBatchEntity tracks lot numbers, manufacturing/expiration dates, supplier info

architectureFeatures.title: Stock Reservation System
architectureFeatures.description: Prevents overselling by reserving stock during order processing with automatic release

architectureFeatures.title: Movement Audit Trail
architectureFeatures.description: All stock adjustments and transfers tracked via InventoryMovementEntity

architectureFeatures.title: Low-Stock Alerts
architectureFeatures.description: InventoryAlertEntity generates alerts when stock falls below reorder level

architectureFeatures.title: RabbitMQ Messaging
architectureFeatures.description: Publishes inventory events to RabbitMQ (inconsistency: other services use Kafka)

architectureFeatures.title: Platform ingress / TLS
architectureFeatures.description: No bundled Nginx in this repo — terminate HTTPS at Kubernetes Ingress, cloud LB, or reuse sibling services’ nginx/ pattern

architectureFeatures.title: Hexagonal Architecture
architectureFeatures.description: Clean separation between domain logic and infrastructure with ports and adapters

architectureFeatures.title: Rich Domain Model
architectureFeatures.description: Order aggregate encapsulates business rules, validation, and state transitions

architectureFeatures.title: Event-Driven
architectureFeatures.description: Domain events enable loose coupling between services

architectureFeatures.title: Role-Based Security
architectureFeatures.description: Fine-grained access control with three distinct roles

architectureFeatures.title: Observability
architectureFeatures.description: Comprehensive monitoring with Actuator, Admin, and ELK stack

architectureFeatures.title: API-First Design
architectureFeatures.description: OpenAPI annotations and Swagger UI for interactive documentation

architectureFeatures.title: Dual Aggregate Roots
architectureFeatures.description: Payment and Sale aggregates with separate lifecycles and state machines

architectureFeatures.title: Domain Events
architectureFeatures.description: PaymentCompletedEvent and PaymentFailedEvent for loose coupling

architectureFeatures.title: Port-Adapter Architecture
architectureFeatures.description: Clean separation between domain logic and infrastructure with StripeGatewayPort

architectureFeatures.title: Strongly-Typed IDs
architectureFeatures.description: PaymentID, OrderID, CustomerID value objects prevent ID confusion

architectureFeatures.title: Factory Methods
architectureFeatures.description: create(), fromPayment(), reconstruct() methods encapsulate object creation

architectureFeatures.title: Value Objects
architectureFeatures.description: Money, PaymentGatewayRef, RefundInfo encapsulate domain concepts

architectureFeatures.title: Clean separation between API, use-cases, and adapters
architectureFeatures.description: Package layout keeps transport/infrastructure concerns out of core logic.

architectureFeatures.title: Composable observability stack
architectureFeatures.description: Prometheus, Loki, and Grafana included in local compose stack.

architectureFeatures.title: Secure-by-default write API
architectureFeatures.description: Explicit role checks and deny-all fallback in security chain.

architectureFeatures.title: Soft delete awareness

architectureFeatures.title: Audit logging hook
architectureFeatures.description: `audit.log` in YAML + `libs_kernel` audit logger configuration present — tune excluded paths per profile.

architectureFeatures.title: Event consumption
architectureFeatures.description: Listens user.created | user.updated | user.deleted; manual ack — failure path risky without DLQ.

architectureFeatures.title: Observability hooks
architectureFeatures.description: Actuator + Prometheus registry + Loki appender.

requestFlow.title: Client Request → Nginx
requestFlow.description: User sends HTTPS request to Nginx on :443. Nginx terminates TLS and load-balances to an address-service replica (least_conn)

requestFlow.title: JWT Authentication
requestFlow.description: Request forwarded by Nginx to address-service. JwtAuthenticationFilter validates Bearer token and sets SecurityContext with AuthUserDetails

requestFlow.title: Rate Limit Check
requestFlow.description: RateLimitAspect checks Redis for rate limit compliance (STANDARD or SENSITIVE profile)

requestFlow.title: Controller Handling
requestFlow.description: UserAddressController or AddressAdminController processes request with role validation

requestFlow.title: Service Layer
requestFlow.description: AddressService orchestrates business logic: validation, mapping, and repository calls

requestFlow.title: Data Persistence
requestFlow.description: AddressRepository saves/retrieves data from PostgreSQL via Spring Data JPA

requestFlow.title: Response Wrapping
requestFlow.description: ResponseWrapper.success/created/updated wraps result and returns to client

requestFlow.title: Client Request → Nginx
requestFlow.description: Client sends HTTPS request to Nginx on :443. Nginx terminates TLS and load-balances to an auth-service replica (least_conn)

requestFlow.title: Rate Limit Check
requestFlow.description: Request forwarded by Nginx to auth-service. RateLimitAspect checks Redis for rate limit compliance (SENSITIVE: 10/min)

requestFlow.title: Use Case Orchestration
requestFlow.description: UseCasesOrquestrator delegates to LoginUseCase with LoginCommand

requestFlow.title: gRPC User Lookup
requestFlow.description: UserServiceGrpcClient retrieves user details from user-service via gRPC

requestFlow.title: Token Generation
requestFlow.description: TokenFactory creates ACCESS + REFRESH JWTs via TokenManager

requestFlow.title: Session Storage
requestFlow.description: RedisSessionRepository stores refresh token in Redis

requestFlow.title: Event Publishing
requestFlow.description: UserEventProducer publishes UserLoginEvent to Kafka

requestFlow.title: Response
requestFlow.description: SessionResponse with access/refresh tokens returned to client

requestFlow.title: Client Request → Nginx
requestFlow.description: User sends HTTPS request to Nginx on :443. Nginx terminates TLS and load-balances to a cart-service replica (least_conn)

requestFlow.title: JWT Authentication
requestFlow.description: Request forwarded by Nginx to cart-service. JwtAuthenticationFilter validates token and sets SecurityContext with userId

requestFlow.title: Controller Handling
requestFlow.description: UserCartController or CartManagerController processes request

requestFlow.title: Use Case Execution
requestFlow.description: CartQueryUseCaseImpl or CartCommandUseCaseImpl executes business logic

requestFlow.title: Domain Logic
requestFlow.description: Cart aggregate root handles business rules (max 100 items, price calculation)

requestFlow.title: Data Persistence/Cache
requestFlow.description: CartRepositoryImpl retrieves/saves via CartJpaRepository with PostgreSQL

requestFlow.title: Response
requestFlow.description: CartResponse returned via CartResponseMapper

requestFlow.title: Client Request to Nginx
requestFlow.description: External request arrives on HTTPS :443

requestFlow.title: Forward to Employee Service
requestFlow.description: Nginx forwards to internal employee-service:8081

requestFlow.title: Client Request
requestFlow.description: Order Service sends request to reserve stock at /api/v2/inventories/{id}/stock/reservations

requestFlow.title: Rate Limit Check
requestFlow.description: GlobalRateLimitFilter checks Redis for rate limit compliance

requestFlow.title: Controller Handling
requestFlow.description: InventoryReservationController processes reservation request

requestFlow.title: Use Case Execution
requestFlow.description: ReservationUseCase reserves stock, checks availability

requestFlow.title: Data Persistence
requestFlow.description: StockReservation saved to PostgreSQL via JpaStockReservationRepository

requestFlow.title: Event Publishing
requestFlow.description: Inventory event published to RabbitMQ (PLACEHOLDER: verify)

requestFlow.title: Response
requestFlow.description: ReservationId returned to client

requestFlow.title: Client → Nginx
requestFlow.description: HTTPS on :443 to Nginx; TLS terminates at edge. Nginx forwards HTTP to order-service:8080 (least_conn across replicas)

requestFlow.title: Security Filter
requestFlow.description: Spring Security validates JWT token and checks role-based permissions (CUSTOMER, EMPLOYEE, ADMIN)

requestFlow.title: Controller Handling
requestFlow.description: Appropriate controller (SaleOrderController, UserOrderController) receives request and validates input

requestFlow.title: Application Service
requestFlow.description: OrderApplicationFacade routes to appropriate command or query handler

requestFlow.title: Domain Logic
requestFlow.description: Order aggregate root executes business logic with validation and state transitions

requestFlow.title: Persistence
requestFlow.description: OrderRepositoryImpl persists changes via JPA to PostgreSQL, with Redis caching

requestFlow.title: Response
requestFlow.description: ResponseWrapper with data is returned to client with appropriate HTTP status

requestFlow.title: Initiate Payment (via Nginx)
requestFlow.description: Browser uses HTTPS :443 to payment-nginx; Nginx proxies HTTP to payment-service:8080. POST /api/v1/payments with orderId, customerId, amount

requestFlow.title: Create Payment Aggregate
requestFlow.description: Payment.create() builds Payment in PENDING state with validation

requestFlow.title: Create Stripe PaymentIntent
requestFlow.description: StripeGatewayAdapter.createPaymentIntent() - ⚠️ STUB, returns null!

requestFlow.title: Return clientSecret
requestFlow.description: PaymentResponse with clientSecret sent to frontend

requestFlow.title: Frontend Confirms Payment
requestFlow.description: Frontend calls stripe.confirmPayment(clientSecret) which calls Stripe

requestFlow.title: Stripe Sends Webhook
requestFlow.description: Stripe calls POST /api/v1/webhooks/stripe with event data

requestFlow.title: Verify & Process Webhook
requestFlow.description: StripeWebhookVerifier validates signature, PaymentApplicationService handles event

requestFlow.title: Complete Payment & Create Sale
requestFlow.description: Payment.complete() → PaymentCompletedEvent → Sale.fromPayment()

requestFlow.title: Client → Nginx
requestFlow.description: product-nginx terminates TLS; forwards HTTP to product-service (upstream product_backend)

requestFlow.title: Client → Nginx → Spring
requestFlow.description: TLS terminates at store-nginx; upstream balances store-service containers on :8080.

requestFlow.title: Security filter
requestFlow.description: JWT parsed when present; GET store routes allowed anonymously by SecurityConfig.

requestFlow.title: Use case → DB / cache
requestFlow.description: Reads consult Redis after miss; writes evict cache regions then commit to Postgres.

requestFlow.title: Client → Nginx
requestFlow.description: TLS on 443 (self-signed certs in dev).

requestFlow.title: Nginx → Spring Boot
requestFlow.description: Proxy to upstream user_backend (user-service:8080).

requestFlow.title: Security filter
requestFlow.description: JWT validated; ROLE_* for manager routes.

eventFlow.title: Address Created Event (Future)

eventFlow.title: 2FA Enabled/Disabled
eventFlow.description: TwoFactorEnabledEvent/TwoFactorDisabledEvent → Kafka → auth.two-factor-* topics

eventFlow.title: Product Event Received

eventFlow.title: Product Event Handled

eventFlow.title: Cart Updated
eventFlow.description: Cart items updated in PostgreSQL with new product data

eventFlow.title: PLACEHOLDER: Cart Purchased
eventFlow.description: CartPurchasedEvent should be published to Kafka when cart is cleared after successful order

eventFlow.title: Employee Domain Events
eventFlow.description: Service may emit/consume events via Kafka depending on integration flow

eventFlow.title: Inventory Created
eventFlow.description: When new inventory created, event published to RabbitMQ

eventFlow.title: Stock Adjusted
eventFlow.description: When stock adjusted, InventoryMovement tracked, event published

eventFlow.title: Low-Stock Alert
eventFlow.description: When stock falls below reorder level, InventoryAlert created

eventFlow.title: Domain Event Creation
eventFlow.description: Order aggregate creates OrderCreatedEvent or OrderStatusChangedEvent

eventFlow.title: Event Publishing
eventFlow.description: EventPublisher port publishes event to event bus (to be implemented)

eventFlow.title: Event Handling
eventFlow.description: Other services (notification, inventory) react to events asynchronously

eventFlow.title: Side Effects
eventFlow.description: Notifications sent, inventory updated, analytics recorded based on events

eventFlow.title: PaymentCompletedEvent Published
eventFlow.description: SpringEventPublisherAdapter publishes event via Spring ApplicationEventPublisher

eventFlow.title: Sale Created
eventFlow.description: Application layer listens to event and creates Sale aggregate from completed Payment

eventFlow.title: Other Services Notified
eventFlow.description: Order Service can listen to event to update order status to CONFIRMED

eventFlow.title: Product Service → Kafka
eventFlow.description: Create/update/delete operations publish product lifecycle events for downstream services.

eventFlow.title: Status change (intended)
eventFlow.description: Domain emits StoreStatusChangedEvent → **StoreEventPublisherImpl logs only**; no broker produce yet.

eventFlow.title: Kafka → UserEventConsumer
eventFlow.description: JSON deserialized to domain events; handler invoked; acknowledge() on success only.

eventFlow.title: Failure path
eventFlow.description: **Dangerous**: no DLQ — exception leaves message unacked (potential poison retry).

decisions.title: Adopt Nginx Edge Proxy
decisions.problem: Need HTTPS endpoint and scalable routing without changing service internals
decisions.solution: Add Nginx in docker-compose with TLS termination and least_conn upstream
decisions.outcome: Stable HTTPS ingress and horizontal scaling path for employee-service
alternatives: Direct service port exposure without load balancing

decisions.title: Hexagonal Architecture with DDD
decisions.problem: Need to isolate business logic from infrastructure and support multiple adapters
decisions.solution: Adopted hexagonal architecture with ports (interfaces) in domain layer and adapters in infrastructure layer
decisions.outcome: Clean separation of concerns, testable domain logic, easy adapter swapping

decisions.title: Java 23 with Records and Pattern Matching
decisions.problem: Need modern language features for concise, type-safe DTOs and value objects
decisions.solution: Used Java 23 with records for DTOs (CreateOrderRequest, OrderResponse) and modern language features
decisions.outcome: Concise code, immutable DTOs, pattern matching for type-safe operations

decisions.title: Spring Boot 3.3.2 with Spring Cloud
decisions.problem: Need a mature framework for building microservices with cloud-native features
decisions.solution: Chose Spring Boot 3.3.2 with Spring Cloud 2023.0.3 for dependency management and cloud integrations
decisions.outcome: Rich ecosystem, easy integration with Spring Cloud services, extensive community support

decisions.title: PostgreSQL with Flyway Migrations
decisions.problem: Need reliable relational storage with versioned schema management
decisions.solution: PostgreSQL 15 as primary database with Flyway for database migrations
decisions.outcome: ACID transactions, JSON support, reliable schema versioning across environments

decisions.title: Redis for Caching
decisions.problem: Need to improve read performance for frequently accessed data
decisions.solution: Integrated Redis with Spring Cache abstraction, using decorator pattern for transparent caching
decisions.outcome: Reduced database load, faster response times for cached data

decisions.title: OpenSearch + Logstash + Dashboards (ELK Stack)
decisions.problem: Need centralized logging and search capabilities for troubleshooting and analytics
decisions.solution: Deployed OpenSearch 2.9.0 with Logstash 8.11.0 and OpenSearch Dashboards for log aggregation
decisions.outcome: Centralized logs, powerful search, visualization dashboards for monitoring

decisions.title: Order State Machine via Enum
decisions.problem: Need to enforce valid order status transitions with clear business rules
decisions.solution: OrderStatus enum with canTransitionTo() method defining all valid state transitions
decisions.outcome: Simple, type-safe state transitions, easy to understand and modify

decisions.title: Shared Kernel Library
decisions.problem: Need common utilities (ResponseWrapper, PageResponse, RateLimit) across microservices
decisions.solution: Created shared-kernel library (io.github.alexisTrejo11:shared-kernel:1.0.0) with common abstractions
decisions.outcome: Consistent patterns across services, reduced duplication, easier maintenance

decisions.title: Dual Aggregate Roots (Payment + Sale)
decisions.problem: Need to separate payment processing lifecycle from confirmed business transaction representation
decisions.solution: Payment aggregate handles payment lifecycle; Sale aggregate auto-generated from completed payments
alternatives: Single Payment entity with sale flag
decisions.outcome: Clear separation of concerns, Sale represents confirmed business transaction, easier auditing

decisions.title: Java 23 with Records and Pattern Matching
decisions.problem: Need modern language features for concise, type-safe DTOs and value objects
decisions.solution: Used Java 23 with records for DTOs and modern language features
decisions.outcome: Concise code, immutable DTOs, pattern matching for type-safe operations

decisions.title: Spring Boot 3.3.2 with Spring Cloud
decisions.problem: Need a mature framework for building microservices with cloud-native features
decisions.solution: Chose Spring Boot 3.3.2 with Spring Cloud 2023.0.3 for dependency management
decisions.outcome: Rich ecosystem, easy integration with Spring Cloud services, extensive community support

decisions.title: PostgreSQL with Flyway Migrations
decisions.problem: Need reliable relational storage with versioned schema management
decisions.solution: PostgreSQL as primary database with Flyway for migrations, JPA ddl-auto: validate
decisions.outcome: ACID transactions, JSON support, reliable schema versioning, Hibernate only validates

decisions.title: Stripe Gateway Adapter (STUB)
decisions.problem: Need to integrate with Stripe for payment processing but implementation pending
decisions.solution: Created StripeGatewayAdapter implementing StripeGatewayPort - currently a STUB returning null/empty
decisions.outcome: ⚠️ STUB - needs actual Stripe SDK integration, all methods return null!

decisions.title: Spring Events for Domain Events
decisions.problem: Need to publish domain events for in-process handling (Sale creation from Payment)
decisions.solution: SpringEventPublisherAdapter implements PaymentEventPublisher port using Spring ApplicationEventPublisher
alternatives: Kafka (added as dependency but not implemented)
decisions.outcome: Synchronous in-process events, easy to migrate to Kafka later (only adapter changes)

decisions.title: Strongly-Typed Value Objects
decisions.problem: Need to prevent ID confusion and encapsulate monetary calculations with currency
decisions.solution: Created PaymentID, OrderID, CustomerID, Money, PaymentGatewayRef, RefundInfo value objects
decisions.outcome: Type safety, encapsulates validation, prevents ID mix-ups, clearer domain model

decisions.title: Kafka Dependency (Not Implemented)
decisions.problem: Future need for event streaming and async communication between microservices
decisions.solution: Added spring-kafka dependency in build.gradle but NO implementation yet
decisions.outcome: ⚠️ Dependency added but no code - compile-time dependency with no runtime usage

decisions.title: Use decorator instead of cache annotations on controllers
decisions.problem: Caching concerns should not leak into transport adapters.
decisions.solution: Applied cache wrappers around ProductQueryUseCases and ProductCommandUseCases.
decisions.outcome: Core API contracts stay stable while caching remains replaceable.
alternatives: Use @Cacheable annotations directly on controller/service methods

decisions.title: Soft delete with restore endpoint
decisions.problem: Need recoverable deletion and auditability for product records.
decisions.solution: Use deleted_at column + SQLDelete + restore flow.
decisions.outcome: Operational safety for accidental deletes.

decisions.title: PostgreSQL + JSONB schedule
decisions.problem: Weekly hours and exceptions vary per store.
decisions.solution: Persist `schedule_config` as JSONB with GIN index for optional query patterns.
decisions.outcome: Flexible schema; validate at application layer.

decisions.title: Redis for cache (not session)
decisions.problem: Repeated search and GET by id/code.
decisions.solution: Spring Cache abstraction with JSON values; 30m TTL.
decisions.outcome: Lower DB load; **watch serialization security**.

decisions.title: Shared kernel JWT
decisions.problem: Consistent auth across microservices.
decisions.solution: Reuse libs_kernel JwtAuthenticationFilter + handlers.
decisions.outcome: Centralized parsing; issuer/secret must match token producer (e.g. auth-service).
alternatives: Spring Authorization Server embedded in user-service

decisions.title: JPA over raw SQL
decisions.problem: Relational persistence with schema migrations.
decisions.solution: Spring Data JPA + Flyway in hardened profiles.
decisions.outcome: **Dev profile** disables Flyway and uses ddl-auto update — inconsistent with Docker profile intent.

decisions.title: gRPC stubs without server lifecycle
decisions.problem: Fast inter-service queries.
decisions.solution: Protobuf contract + blocking stub impl class.
decisions.outcome: **Not production-ready** until ServerBuilder lifecycle added or spring-grpc adopted.

codeExamples.title: Postal Code Validator Factory Pattern
codeExamples.description: Factory pattern implementation that creates country-specific postal code validators based on ISO country code

files.name: PostalCodeValidatorFactory.java
files.explanation: Factory uses Spring's dependency injection to collect all PostalCodeValidator implementations and stores them in a map keyed by country code. getValidator() returns the appropriate validator or falls back to DefaultPostalCodeValidator.

files.name: PostalCodeValidator.java
files.explanation: Strategy interface that defines the contract for all postal code validators.

files.name: USPostalCodeValidator.java
files.explanation: Example of a concrete strategy implementation for US ZIP codes.

codeExamples.title: Redis Rate Limiting with AOP Aspect
codeExamples.description: Aspect-oriented programming implementation for rate limiting API endpoints using Redis as distributed counter

files.name: RateLimitAspect.java
files.explanation: AOP aspect that intercepts methods annotated with @RateLimit and checks Redis-based rate limiting before proceeding with the method execution.

files.name: RedisRateLimiter.java
files.explanation: Redis-backed rate limiter using token bucket algorithm. Uses atomic increment and TTL for time window.

codeExamples.title: Address Service CRUD Operations
codeExamples.description: Core service class demonstrating transaction management, validation, and business logic for address operations

files.name: AddressService.java
files.explanation: Service layer with @Transactional methods for create, update, and delete operations. Includes business logic for address limits, default address handling, and soft delete.

files.name: Address.java (DTO)
files.explanation: Immutable DTO using Java record. fromEntity() factory method converts JPA entity to API response object.

codeExamples.title: Dual Controller Architecture (User vs Admin)
codeExamples.description: Separate controllers for user self-service and admin operations with different authorization requirements

files.name: UserAddressController.java
files.explanation: User controller with @SecurityRequirement for JWT auth. Users can only access their own addresses. @RateLimit annotation applies rate limiting profiles.

files.name: AddressAdminController.java
files.explanation: Admin controller without user-specific restrictions. Admin can access any address and manage addresses for any user.

codeExamples.title: DDD UseCasesOrchestrator Pattern
codeExamples.description: Central orchestrator implementing 4 port interfaces (AuthUseCases, LogoutUseCases, PasswordUseCases, RegisterUseCases, TwoFaConfigUseCases) following Domain-Driven Design

files.name: UseCasesOrquestrator.java
files.explanation: Orchestrator pattern: single entry point delegating to specific use case implementations. Implements multiple port interfaces for clean API.

codeExamples.title: TokenFactory - Multi-Type Token Creation
codeExamples.description: Factory creating 4 token types: ACCESS (JWT), REFRESH (JWT), ACTIVATION (numeric), TWO_FA (numeric) using JJWT library

files.name: TokenFactory.java
files.explanation: Factory pattern with switch expression for different token types. JWTs signed with HMAC-SHA key, numeric tokens for activation/2FA.

files.name: TokenType.java
files.explanation: Enum defining the 4 token types supported by the auth service.

codeExamples.title: Kafka Event Publishing
codeExamples.description: UserEventProducer publishes 8+ event types to Kafka topics with CompletableFuture for non-blocking operations

files.name: UserEventProducer.java
files.explanation: Uses KafkaTemplate with CompletableFuture for async publishing. Events include: UserCreated, UserUpdated, UserDeleted, UserRegistered, UserLogin, PasswordChanged, AccountActivated, TwoFactorEnabled/Disabled.

codeExamples.title: gRPC Client for User-Service
codeExamples.description: UserServiceGrpcClient communicates with user-service via Protobuf/gRPC for user CRUD operations

files.name: UserServiceGrpcClient.java
files.explanation: Uses gRPC blocking stub for synchronous calls. Protobuf messages defined in .proto files. Maps between Protobuf and domain objects using UserGrpcMapper.

files.name: UserGrpcMapper.java
files.explanation: Mapper between Protobuf messages and domain objects/DTOs.

codeExamples.title: Redis Session Management
codeExamples.description: RedisSessionRepository manages JWT refresh token sessions with blacklisting support for immediate revocation

files.name: RedisSessionRepository.java
files.path: PLACEHOLDER: Check adapter/output/persistence/ for actual file
files.explanation: Stores refresh token sessions in Redis with TTL matching token expiration. Blacklisting allows immediate session revocation before expiration.

codeExamples.title: Command Pattern for Requests
codeExamples.description: Each request type has a corresponding Command object (SignupCommand, LoginCommand, ChangePasswordCommand) with validation

files.name: SignupCommand.java
files.explanation: Command objects encapsulate request data with domain value objects (Email, Password, PhoneNumber) for validation at the domain level.

files.name: LoginCommand.java
files.explanation: Simple command for login with identifier (email or phone) and password.

codeExamples.title: Full-stack HTTP integration tests
codeExamples.description: AuthEndpointsIntegrationTest boots the Spring context with Testcontainers Redis/Kafka, wires an in-process UserService gRPC server (InMemoryUserGrpcServer), and drives register→activate→login and related flows using real Redis opaque tokens.

files.name: AuthEndpointsIntegrationTest.java
files.explanation: Uses real infrastructure boundaries (Redis keys, Kafka producers, gRPC stub) so regressions in wiring or token handling surface in CI when Docker is available.

codeExamples.title: Cart Aggregate Root Pattern
codeExamples.description: Cart.java is the aggregate root encapsulating items and afterwardsItems with business logic for add/update/remove/clear operations

files.name: Cart.java

codeExamples.title: Redis Cache Configuration
codeExamples.description: Spring Boot cache abstraction with Redis backend, 1-hour TTL for general cache, lettuce connection pool

files.name: application.yml (Redis config)
files.content:
spring:
  data:
    redis:
      host: ${SPRING_REDIS_HOST:localhost}
      port: ${SPRING_REDIS_PORT:6379}
      password: ${SPRING_REDIS_PASSWORD:redispass}
      timeout: 2000ms
      lettuce:
        pool:
          max-active: 8
          max-idle: 8
          min-idle: 0
          max-wait: 1000ms

  cache:
    type: redis
    redis:
      time-to-live: 3600000  # 1 hour
      cache-null-values: false
      enable-statistics: true
files.explanation: Redis configured with environment variable support, lettuce pool sizing, and 1-hour TTL. Cache statistics enabled for monitoring.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.content:
.requestMatchers(HttpMethod.GET, "/api/v2/stores/**").permitAll()
.requestMatchers(HttpMethod.POST, "/api/v2/stores/**").hasAnyRole("ADMIN", "MANAGER")
.requestMatchers(HttpMethod.PUT, "/api/v2/stores/**").hasAnyRole("ADMIN", "MANAGER")
.requestMatchers(HttpMethod.PATCH, "/api/v2/stores/**").hasAnyRole("ADMIN", "MANAGER")
.requestMatchers(HttpMethod.DELETE, "/api/v2/stores/**").hasAnyRole("ADMIN", "MANAGER")

codeExamples.title: Redis CacheManager with Jackson JSON values
codeExamples.description: 30-minute TTL, nulls disabled, custom ObjectMapper including JavaTimeModule and default typing activation.

files.name: CacheConfig.java
files.content:
RedisCacheConfiguration.defaultCacheConfig()
    .entryTtl(Duration.ofMinutes(30))
    .disableCachingNullValues()
objectMapper.activateDefaultTyping(
    objectMapper.getPolymorphicTypeValidator(),
    ObjectMapper.DefaultTyping.NON_FINAL);
files.explanation: Default typing can be unsafe if untrusted data reaches Redis; prefer explicit DTO types or disable typing for hardened deployments.

features.title: Multi-Country Postal Code Validation
features.description: Validates postal codes against country-specific formats using Factory and Strategy patterns. Supports US (ZIP), Mexico (CP), Canada, Spain, and UK with regex-based validation. Extensible design allows adding new countries easily.
highlights: Factory Pattern: PostalCodeValidatorFactory creates appropriate validator based on country code
Strategy Pattern: Each country implements PostalCodeValidator interface
Supports 5+ countries: US, MX, CA, ES, UK
DefaultPostalCodeValidator as fallback for unsupported countries
Format description returned in error messages for client guidance

features.title: Role-Based Address Limits
features.description: Enforces different address limits based on user type: CUSTOMERs can have up to 5 addresses, while EMPLOYEEs are limited to 1 address. Configurable via AddressServiceProperties with @ConfigurationProperties.
highlights: User type auto-determined from JWT role (CUSTOMER/EMPLOYEE)

features.title: Default Address Management
features.description: Allows users to set a default address with automatic handling: first address becomes default automatically, setting a new default resets the previous one. Implemented at database level with resetDefaultAddressForUser().
highlights: First address for user automatically set as default
Only one address can be default per user (database constraint)

features.title: Dual Controller Architecture
features.description: Separate controllers for user self-service (UserAddressController) and admin operations (AddressAdminController). Clear separation of concerns with different authorization requirements and base paths.
highlights: User controller: /api/v2/user/addresses (requires USER or ADMIN role)
Users can only access their own addresses
Admins can manage addresses for any user
Shared AddressService with different method signatures for authorization

codeSnippet.code:
http
    .authorizeHttpRequests(authorize -> authorize
        .requestMatchers("/api/v2/addresses/**").hasRole("ADMIN")
        .requestMatchers("/api/v2/user/addresses/**").hasAnyRole("CUSTOMER", "ADMIN")
        ...

features.title: Redis-Backed Rate Limiting
features.description: Distributed rate limiting using Redis with token bucket algorithm. Applied via AOP aspect with @RateLimit annotation. Two profiles: STANDARD (60/min) for reads, SENSITIVE (10/min) for writes.
highlights: Redis atomic increment for distributed rate counting
Rate limit info endpoint for clients (X-RateLimit-* headers PLACEHOLDER)

features.title: JWT Authentication & Authorization
features.description: Stateless authentication using JWT tokens validated by JwtAuthenticationFilter from shared libs-kernel. Role-based access control with CUSTOMER, EMPLOYEE, and ADMIN roles.
highlights: JwtAuthenticationFilter from shared libs-kernel for token validation
AuthUserDetails provides userId and role in controller methods

features.title: Soft Delete Pattern
features.description: Addresses are never hard-deleted; instead, the 'active' flag is set to false. This preserves data integrity and allows potential recovery. @PrePersist ensures new addresses are active by default.
highlights: active flag (boolean) instead of DELETE FROM database
All queries filter by active=true (findByActiveTrue, findByUserIdAndActiveTrue)
Preserves referential integrity with orders and other entities
Audit trail: address data remains for historical records

features.title: Docker Containerization
features.description: Multi-stage Docker build with Eclipse Temurin JDK 23 for build and JRE for runtime. Non-root user (spring:spring) for security, health checks via Actuator, and SSL/HTTPS on port 8443.
highlights: Multi-stage build: JDK for compilation, JRE for runtime (smaller image)
Non-root user execution (spring:spring) for security best practices
Entrypoint script fixes volume permissions before starting

codeSnippet.code:
# Build stage
FROM eclipse-temurin:23-jdk-alpine AS builder
COPY . /app
RUN ./gradlew bootJar --no-daemon

# Runtime stage
FROM eclipse-temurin:23-jre-alpine
RUN addgroup -S spring && adduser -S spring -G spring
COPY --from=builder /app/build/libs/*.jar app.jar
USER spring
EXPOSE 8443
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

features.title: OpenAPI Documentation (Swagger)
features.description: Comprehensive API documentation using Springdoc OpenAPI 2.6.0. Custom annotations per endpoint with request/response examples. Swagger UI available at /swagger-ui.html.
highlights: Springdoc OpenAPI 2.6.0 integration with Spring Boot 3.3.2
Custom annotations (e.g., @GetMyAddressesAnnotation) with full OpenAPI metadata
JWT Bearer Auth defined in OpenAPI components
Swagger UI at /swagger-ui.html, API docs at /v3/api-docs

features.title: Database Migrations with Flyway
features.description: Versioned database schema management using Flyway. V1__initial_schema.sql creates the addresses table with proper indexes for performance. Supports PostgreSQL with automated migration on startup.
highlights: Indexes on user_id and (user_id, isDefault) for query performance

codeSnippet.code:
CREATE TABLE addresses (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    user_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    street VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    city VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    state VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    country VARCHAR(2) NOT NULL,
    postal_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    additional_details VARCHAR(200),
    is_default BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX idx_user_id ON addresses(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_default ON addresses(user_id, is_default);

Micrometer Prometheus registry enabled for service/JVM metrics
Logback Loki appender enabled for non-test profiles
Grafana data sources provisioned automatically (Prometheus + Loki)

management:

        include: health,info,prometheus
  endpoint:

  metrics:

features.title: GitHub Packages (Shared External Library)
features.description: Consumes the platform shared-kernel artifact from GitHub Packages Maven instead of vendoring Java sources into each microservice. Gradle and Docker builds authenticate with GITHUB_ACTOR and GITHUB_TOKEN to download io.github.alexisTrejo11:shared-kernel at a pinned version.
highlights: Shared APIs: ResponseWrapper, Error, domain exceptions, JWT DTOs, AuditEvent, pagination/mappers (libs_kernel.*)
build.gradle loads .env at configuration time via envOrDotEnv for GitHub Packages credentials
docker-compose build args forward GITHUB_ACTOR and GITHUB_TOKEN into the Gradle builder stage
Published from libs/shared-kernel with maven-publish; bump version in dependencies when releasing

features.title: JWT Access + Refresh Token Management
features.description: Secure token-based authentication with short-lived access tokens (15min) and long-lived refresh tokens (7 days). TokenFactory creates HMAC-SHA signed JWTs with configurable expiration via JJWT 0.11.5.
highlights: ACCESS tokens: short-lived (15min default), contains role, userId, email, name, phoneNumber
REFRESH tokens: long-lived (7 days), used to obtain new access tokens
HMAC-SHA signing with configurable secret key
TokenFactory creates JWTs + numeric tokens (ACTIVATION, TWO_FA)
Claims extraction and validation via Jwts.parserBuilder()

features.title: Two-Factor Authentication (TOTP)
features.description: Enhances security with time-based one-time passwords. Users can enable/disable 2FA, receive validation codes via notification service, and complete 2FA login flow.
highlights: Events: TwoFactorEnabledEvent, TwoFactorDisabledEvent published to Kafka

features.title: OAuth2 Social Login
features.description: Supports authentication via external OAuth2 providers (Google, GitHub, etc.) using Spring OAuth2 Client. CustomOAuth2UserService handles user creation/login.
highlights: ExternalIDs value object stores provider-specific user IDs

features.title: Password Management & Reset Flow
features.description: Comprehensive password handling: change password (authenticated), forgot password (email reset link), reset password (with token), and validate reset token. Uses BCrypt hashing.
highlights: Forgot password: generates ACTIVATION token, sends email via Kafka event
Reset password: validates token, updates password in user-service via gRPC
Change password: requires current password verification
PasswordChangedEvent published to Kafka for audit/logging

features.title: Kafka Event Publishing
features.description: Publishes 8+ domain events to Kafka topics for asynchronous inter-service communication. Uses CompletableFuture for non-blocking publishing with timeout.

features.title: gRPC Client for User-Service
features.description: High-performance Protobuf-based RPC communication with user-service for user CRUD operations. Uses gRPC 1.60.0 and Protobuf 3.25.1.
highlights: UserServiceGrpcClient using blocking stub for sync calls
UserGrpcMapper for Protobuf ↔ Domain object mapping
CRUD operations: getUserByEmail, getUserById, createUser, updateUser, deleteUser
PLACEHOLDER: Add Circuit Breaker (Resilience4j) for fault tolerance

features.title: DDD Ports & Adapters Architecture
features.description: Clean bounded context with ports (interfaces) in core/ports and adapters (implementations) in adapter/output. UseCasesOrquestrator coordinates all use cases.
highlights: Ports: AuthUseCases, LogoutUseCases, PasswordUseCases, RegisterUseCases, TwoFaConfigUseCases

features.title: Redis Session Management
features.description: Refresh token sessions stored in Redis with configurable TTL. Supports session blacklisting for immediate token revocation. Rate limiting also uses Redis.
highlights: Sessions keyed by refresh token with user ID mapping
RedisRateLimiter for distributed rate limiting (token bucket)

features.title: Value Objects Pattern
features.description: Domain values wrapped in objects for validation: Email, Password (BCrypt hashed), UserId, UserRole (enum), Token, SessionId, PhoneNumber, OAuth2Provider, ExternalIDs.
highlights: Email value object validates email format
Password value object wraps BCrypt hashed password

features.title: Multi-Role User Registration
features.description: Supports registration for different user roles: CUSTOMER (default), EMPLOYEE, and ADMIN. Each role has separate registration endpoint with role assignment.
highlights: Role assigned via SignupCommand with UserRole enum
Account activation required via email token (ACTIVATION type)

features.title: HTTP integration tests (Testcontainers)
features.description: Spring Boot tests spin up real Redis and Kafka via Testcontainers and an in-process Netty gRPC UserService with BCrypt validation. Covers registration→activation→login, refresh/logout, password reset, 2FA, and authenticated routes.
highlights: Profiles: integration-test + test (JWT test secret, relaxed rate limits, Logback excludes Loki)
Opaque tokens read from Redis (ACTIVATION, PASSWORD_RESET, TWO_FA) for realistic flows
InMemoryUserGrpcServer implements full UserService contract used by UserServiceGrpcClient
@Testcontainers(disabledWithoutDocker = true) skips when Docker is not installed
