features.description: CartPurchasedEvent is defined but not published to Kafka yet. Should be published when cart is cleared after successful order.
highlights: CartPurchasedEvent - Should be published to cart.purchased topic
Domain events collected in Cart aggregate via domainEvents list
PLACEHOLDER: Event publishing not implemented yet
Would notify other services (e.g., inventory-service) of cart purchase

features.title: Rich JPA Entity Model
features.description: EmployeeEntity with embedded objects (ContactInfo, Address), certifications list (OneToMany), workday schedule as JSONB, and comprehensive enums for roles, types, and status.
highlights: EmployeeTypeEnum: 4 types (FULL_TIME, PART_TIME, CONTRACTOR, INTERN, SEASONAL)
EmployeeStatusEnum: 5 statuses (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ON_LEAVE, SUSPENDED, TERMINATED)
ContactInfoEmbeddable and AddressEmbeddable for complex objects
workdaySchedule as JSONB column for flexible schedule storage
Certifications list with cascade ALL and orphanRemoval
5 database indexes: employeeNumber (unique), status, role, storeId, deletedAt
Audit fields: createdAt, updatedAt, createdBy, lastModifiedBy

features.title: Command/Query Separation (CQS)
features.description: Separate controllers and services for commands (writes) and queries (reads) with dedicated command/query objects.
highlights: EmployeeCommandController: POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE operations
EmployeeQueryController: GET operations (by id, by number, search, exists checks)

Command Objects: CreateEmployeeCommand, UpdateEmployeeCommand, ChangeRoleCommand, ChangeStatusCommand, UpdateCompensationCommand, SuspendEmployeeCommand, ActivateEmployeeCommand, PutOnLeaveCommand, DeleteEmployeeCommand, RestoreEmployeeCommand, AddCertificationCommand
Query Objects: GetEmployeeByIdQuery, GetEmployeeByNumberQuery, SearchEmployeesQuery, CheckEmployeeExistsByIdQuery, CheckEmployeeExistsByNumberQuery
All endpoints use @RateLimit with profiles from libs-kernel

features.title: Rate Limiting with libs-kernel
features.description: Uses @RateLimit annotations from shared libs-kernel library with three profiles: PUBLIC (60/min), STANDARD (60/min), SENSITIVE (10/min).
highlights: PUBLIC profile (60 requests/min): Used for query endpoints (GET)
STANDARD profile (60 requests/min): Used for standard operations (PUT, PATCH for status/role)
SENSITIVE profile (10 requests/min): Used for sensitive operations (POST, DELETE, PATCH for suspend/activate)
Redis-backed rate limiting via libs-kernel RateLimitAspect
All endpoints annotated (unlike cart-service which has none)

features.title: Soft Delete with Audit Trail
features.description: Employees are soft-deleted via deletedAt timestamp, with restore functionality. Complete audit trail with createdBy, lastModifiedBy, createdAt, updatedAt.
highlights: deletedAt timestamp for soft delete (null = active)
markAsDeleted() sets deletedAt and status to TERMINATED
markAsRestored() clears deletedAt and sets status to ACTIVE
createdBy and lastModifiedBy for audit trail

features.title: Comprehensive Enum Types
features.description: Type-safe enums for employee roles (9), types (5), and statuses (5) stored as strings in PostgreSQL using @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING).
highlights: EmployeeRoleEnum: PHARMACIST, PHARMACY_TECHNICIAN, STORE_MANAGER, ASSISTANT_MANAGER, CASHIER, INVENTORY_CLERK, DELIVERY_DRIVER, CUSTOMER_SERVICE_REP, JANITOR
EmployeeTypeEnum: FULL_TIME, PART_TIME, CONTRACTOR, INTERN, SEASONAL
EmployeeStatusEnum: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ON_LEAVE, SUSPENDED, TERMINATED
Stored as strings in PostgreSQL for readability
Easy to add new values without schema changes

codeSnippet.code:
public enum EmployeeRoleEnum {
    PHARMACIST,
    PHARMACY_TECHNICIAN,
    STORE_MANAGER,
    ASSISTANT_MANAGER,
    CASHIER,
    INVENTORY_CLERK,
    DELIVERY_DRIVER,
    CUSTOMER_SERVICE_REP,
    JANITOR
}

features.title: JSONB Workday Schedule Storage
features.description: Flexible workday schedule stored as JSONB in PostgreSQL, allowing complex schedule structures without schema changes.
highlights: Flexible key-value structure for different schedule formats
No schema changes needed for schedule format updates

features.title: Certification Management
features.description: Track employee certifications (pharmacy license, CPR, etc.) with issuance and expiration dates. OneToMany relationship with cascade ALL.
highlights: CertificationEntity with name, issuingOrganization, issueDate, expirationDate
OneToMany relationship from EmployeeEntity with cascade ALL and orphanRemoval
Stored in separate table with foreign key to employees

features.title: Dynamic Search with Specification Pattern
features.description: EmployeeSpecificationBuilder creates dynamic JPA criteria queries for searching employees with multiple optional filters.
highlights: Optional filters: firstName, lastName, role, status, employeeType, storeId
Uses JPA Specification API for type-safe criteria building
Returns Page<EmployeeEntity> for efficient large result sets

codeSnippet.code:
// Builds dynamic JPA Specifications based on search criteria
// Supports filtering by: firstName, lastName, role, status, etc.

features.title: Spring Cloud Config Client
features.description: Centralized configuration management using Spring Cloud Config Client 2023.0.3, reading config from config-server directory.

features.title: Database Migrations with Flyway
features.description: Versioned database schema management using Flyway 10.17.0. V1__create_tables.sql and V2__insert_dummy_data.sql.
highlights: V1__create_tables.sql: Creates employees and certifications tables with indexes
V2__insert_dummy_data.sql: Sample employee data for development
Indexes on employeeNumber (unique), status, role, storeId, deletedAt

features.title: Inventory Management
features.description: Core inventory tracking with total, available, and reserved quantity management. Supports reorder levels, maximum stock levels, and warehouse location tracking.
highlights: Track total, available, and reserved quantities
CQRS pattern with separate query/command models

features.title: Batch Tracking & Expiration Management
features.description: Pharmaceutical-grade batch tracking with lot numbers, manufacturing/expiration dates, supplier info, and storage conditions. Supports batch status lifecycle (ACTIVE, EXPIRED, DAMAGED, QUARANTINED).

features.title: Stock Reservation System
features.description: Temporary stock reservation system for order processing. Supports reserve, confirm, release, and cancel operations with reason tracking.

features.title: Stock Movements (Adjustments & Transfers)
features.description: Comprehensive stock movement tracking with adjustments (damage, loss, correction) and transfers between inventory locations. Full audit trail via InventoryMovement entities.

features.title: Expiration Alerts & Batch Lifecycle
features.description: Proactive expiration monitoring with configurable day thresholds. Batch lifecycle management including mark-as-expired, mark-as-damaged, and quarantine operations.

features.title: RabbitMQ Event Publishing
features.description: Asynchronous event publishing via RabbitMQ (AMQP). Publishes inventory events for other services. NOTE: This creates inconsistency as other services use Kafka.

features.title: Redis Caching
features.description: Spring Cache abstraction with Redis backend. 1-hour TTL for general cache with statistics enabled. Used for inventory queries and rate limiting.

features.title: Rate Limiting
features.description: Global and endpoint-specific rate limiting using custom RateLimit filter. Protects against abuse with configurable request thresholds.

features.title: Spring Boot Actuator & Monitoring
features.description: Comprehensive monitoring with Actuator endpoints (health, info, metrics, env, prometheus). Exposes Redis, DB, and disk space health indicators.
highlights: Health checks for Redis, DB, disk space

features.title: Flyway Database Migrations
features.description: Database schema versioning with Flyway. Currently disabled in configuration. Supports PostgreSQL with public schema.

features.title: OpenAPI / Swagger Documentation
features.description: Auto-generated API documentation with SpringDoc OpenAPI. Exposes Swagger UI and API docs endpoints for easy API exploration.
highlights: Alphabetical sorting for tags and operations

features.title: Integration tests (Spring profile test)
features.description: End-to-end REST tests with H2, real JWT validation (JwtAuthenticationFilter), Redis/Kafka excluded, rate limiting off. InventoryApiIntegrationTest + IntegrationTestJwtSupport.
highlights: H2 MODE=PostgreSQL; Hibernate ddl-auto create-drop; Flyway disabled
Bearer JWT built with same jwt.secret as test YAML (no mocked Security)

features.title: Create Order
features.description: Create new orders supporting both delivery and store pickup methods with item validation, duplicate detection, and automatic ID generation
highlights: Supports delivery and pickup order creation via single endpoint
Validates items for duplicates and null values
Enforces currency consistency across service fee, tax, and items

features.title: Search Orders
features.description: Paginated and filtered order search with dynamic criteria including status, delivery method, date ranges, and user ID
highlights: Filter by status, delivery method, date range, user ID

features.title: Order Status Management
features.description: Complete order lifecycle management with state machine validation, supporting confirm, prepare, ship, deliver, pickup, cancel, and return operations
highlights: 9 order statuses with validated state transitions
Automatic timestamp tracking on status changes

features.title: Customer Order Access
features.description: Dedicated API for customers to view their own orders with pagination, filtering by status, and detailed order information
highlights: Customers can only access their own orders (enforced by userID)
Detailed order view with items and delivery/pickup info

features.title: Delivery Address Management
features.description: Manage delivery addresses with CRUD operations, validation against order state, and support for multiple addresses per user
highlights: Create, read, update, delete delivery addresses
Prevent deletion of addresses in use by active orders
Support for building types (HOUSE, APARTMENT, BUSINESS)

features.title: Order Deletion
features.description: Soft and hard delete capabilities for orders with proper validation and audit trail
highlights: Soft delete preserves data for audit purposes
Hard delete for complete removal (admin only)

features.title: API Rate Limiting
features.description: Configurable rate limiting with global and per-profile limits to protect against abuse and ensure fair usage
highlights: Global rate limit: 5000 requests per hour
Per-profile limits: standard (100/60s), sensitive (5/300s), public (1000/60s), admin (50/1s)

features.title: Redis Caching
features.description: Redis-based caching layer for frequently accessed data with decorator pattern implementation

features.title: Domain Event Publishing
features.description: Publish domain events for order creation and status changes to enable event-driven architecture and async processing
highlights: OrderCreatedEvent with orderId, userID, totalAmount, createdAt
Events enable integration with other microservices

features.title: Role-Based Security
features.description: Comprehensive security with HTTPS enforcement, CORS configuration, and role-based access control for customers, employees, and admins
highlights: Roles: CUSTOMER (own orders), EMPLOYEE (sale orders), ADMIN (all operations)
CORS configured for localhost:3000 and production domain
Bearer token authentication via Spring Security

features.title: Observability and Monitoring
features.description: Comprehensive monitoring with Spring Boot Actuator, Spring Boot Admin, and ELK stack for centralized logging
highlights: Spring Boot Actuator exposes health, metrics, env, loggers endpoints
Spring Boot Admin for centralized management
ELK stack: OpenSearch, Logstash, OpenSearch Dashboards

features.title: OpenAPI Documentation
features.description: Automated API documentation with Swagger UI and OpenAPI 3 annotations for all endpoints
highlights: Custom operation annotations for consistent documentation

features.title: Database Migrations
features.description: Versioned database schema management with Flyway for reliable and repeatable migrations

features.title: Initiate Payment
features.description: Creates a payment in PENDING state, registers a Stripe PaymentIntent, transitions to PROCESSING and returns the clientSecret needed by the frontend to confirm payment.
highlights: Creates Payment aggregate in PENDING state
Registers Stripe PaymentIntent via gateway adapter
Returns clientSecret for frontend Stripe.js confirmation

features.title: Process Refund
features.description: Processes full or partial refunds for completed payments. Calls Stripe to issue refund, then updates Payment and Sale status accordingly.
highlights: Supports full refunds (amount=null) and partial refunds
Updates Payment status to REFUNDED or PARTIALLY_REFUNDED

features.title: Stripe Webhook Handler
features.description: Receives and processes Stripe webhook events with signature verification. Handles payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed, and charge.refunded events.
highlights: Handles payment_intent.succeeded → Payment COMPLETED → Sale CREATED
Handles charge.refunded → Payment REFUNDED → Sale updated
Always returns 200 to prevent Stripe retries

features.title: Payment Query Operations
features.description: Retrieve payments by ID, order ID, or customer ID. Supports single payment lookup and customer payment history.
highlights: Results ordered by creation date descending

features.title: Sale Query Operations
features.description: Read-only sale records automatically created from completed payments. Sales represent confirmed business transactions.
highlights: GET /api/v1/sales/{saleId} - Single sale lookup with net amount
Includes confirmed, refunded, and cancelled sales

features.title: Payment State Machine
features.description: Payment aggregate with 6 states and validated transitions. Enforces business rules for payment lifecycle.
highlights: Business rule validation in each state transition

features.title: Sale State Machine
features.description: Sale aggregate with 4 states, automatically generated from completed payments. Represents confirmed business transactions.
highlights: Tracks refunded amount and net amount

features.title: Domain Events for Event-Driven Architecture
features.description: PaymentCompletedEvent and PaymentFailedEvent enable loose coupling. SpringEventPublisherAdapter publishes events for in-process handling.
highlights: PaymentFailedEvent allows other services to handle failures
Extensible for Kafka/RabbitMQ migration (only adapter needs change)

highlights: Money - Monetary amounts with currency support
PaymentGatewayRef - Tracks Stripe PaymentIntent and Charge IDs
RefundInfo - Captures refund details (amount, reason, gateway ID)

features.title: Database Migrations
features.description: Versioned database schema management with Flyway for reliable and repeatable migrations.
highlights: Flyway core and PostgreSQL support in build.gradle

features.title: OpenAPI Documentation
features.description: Automated API documentation with Swagger UI and OpenAPI 3 annotations for all endpoints.
highlights: Response examples and status codes documented

GET by id/sku/barcode supports public read access

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage Docker build with Eclipse Temurin JDK 23 for build and JRE for runtime
dockerFiles.content:
# Dockerfile (in the root of address-service)
FROM eclipse-temurin:23-jdk-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Copy gradle wrapper and build files
COPY gradlew .
COPY gradle gradle
COPY gradle.properties .
COPY build.gradle .
COPY settings.gradle .

# Give execution permissions to gradlew
RUN chmod +x gradlew

# Download dependencies (cache layer)
RUN ./gradlew dependencies --no-daemon

# Copy source code
COPY src src

# Build the application
RUN ./gradlew bootJar --no-daemon

# Execution stage
FROM eclipse-temurin:23-jre-alpine

WORKDIR /app

# Create non-root user for security
RUN addgroup -S spring && adduser -S spring -G spring

# Copy the built JAR
COPY --from=builder /app/build/libs/*.jar app.jar

# Create directory for logs and config
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs /app/config

# Give permissions to the user
RUN chown -R spring:spring /app

# Create entrypoint script to fix mounted volume permissions
RUN echo '#!/bin/sh' > /app/entrypoint.sh && \
    echo 'chown -R spring:spring /app/logs 2>/dev/null || true' >> /app/entrypoint.sh && \
    echo 'exec su-exec spring:spring java -Djava.security.egd=file:/dev/./urandom -Dspring.profiles.active=${SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE:-docker} -Dlogging.file.path=/app/logs -jar /app/app.jar "$@"' >> /app/entrypoint.sh && \
    chmod +x /app/entrypoint.sh

# Install su-exec for proper user switching
USER root
RUN apk add --no-cache su-exec

# Health check (using HTTPS on 8443)
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --quiet --tries=1 --spider --no-check-certificate https://localhost:8443/actuator/health || exit 1

# Expose port (HTTPS)
EXPOSE 8443

# Entry command (run as root to fix permissions, then drop to spring user)
ENTRYPOINT ["/app/entrypoint.sh"]

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage Docker build with shared-kernel library compilation and gRPC support
dockerFiles.content:
# Dockerfile (in the root of auth-service)
FROM eclipse-temurin:23-jdk-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Copy gradle wrapper and parent build files
COPY gradlew .
COPY gradle gradle
COPY gradle.properties .
COPY build.gradle .
COPY settings.gradle .

# Remove the org.gradle.java.home property as it points to SDKMAN path
RUN sed -i '/org.gradle.java.home/d' gradle.properties

# Copy shared-kernel library
COPY libs/shared-kernel libs/shared-kernel

# Copy auth-service build file
COPY auth-service/build.gradle auth-service/

# Give execution permissions to gradlew
RUN chmod +x gradlew

# Build shared-kernel and publish to local Maven
RUN ./gradlew :libs:shared-kernel:publishToMavenLocal --no-daemon

# Download auth-service dependencies
RUN ./gradlew :auth-service:dependencies --no-daemon

# Copy auth-service source code
COPY auth-service/src auth-service/src

# Build the application
RUN ./gradlew :auth-service:bootJar --no-daemon

# Execution stage
FROM eclipse-temurin:23-jre-alpine

WORKDIR /app

# Create non-root user for security
RUN addgroup -S spring && adduser -S spring -G spring

# Copy the built JAR
COPY --from=builder /app/auth-service/build/libs/*.jar app.jar

# Copy the SSL keystore
COPY auth-service/src/main/resources/keystore.p12 /app/keystore.p12

# Create directory for logs and config
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs /app/config

# Give permissions to the user
RUN chown -R spring:spring /app

# Create entrypoint script
RUN echo '#!/bin/sh' > /app/entrypoint.sh && \
    echo 'chown -R spring:spring /app/logs 2>/dev/null || true' >> /app/entrypoint.sh && \
    echo 'exec su-exec spring:spring java $JAVA_OPTS -Djava.security.egd=file:/dev/./urandom -Dspring.profiles.active=${SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE:-docker} -Dlogging.file.path=/app/logs -jar /app/app.jar "$@"' >> /app/entrypoint.sh && \
    chmod +x /app/entrypoint.sh

# Install runtime utilities
USER root
RUN apk add --no-cache su-exec wget

# Health check (using HTTPS on 8443)
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --quiet --tries=1 --spider --no-check-certificate https://localhost:8443/actuator/health || exit 1

# Expose port (HTTPS)
EXPOSE 8443

# Entry command
ENTRYPOINT ["/app/entrypoint.sh"]

name: Marketing Company API
description: Minimalist GraphQL backend for CRM and marketing operations. Hexagonal architecture on Java 23 and Spring Boot, deployed on AWS (EC2, RDS, Upstash Redis) with external Kafka consumption and Prometheus-ready observability.
techStack: Redis (Upstash in production, local/Docker for dev)

problemStatement.problemTitle: Unified CRM and marketing data without a monolithic frontend lock-in
problemStatement.problemDescription: Marketing agencies and CRM teams need a single backend that models customers, sales pipelines, campaigns, and attribution — while staying API-first, secure, and deployable to cloud infrastructure without coupling to a specific UI framework.
problemList: Fragmented CRM and marketing data across spreadsheets and ad-hoc tools
Need for JWT-authenticated, rate-limited APIs suitable for multiple clients
Session and cache requirements that differ between local Docker and cloud (Upstash)
Operational need for health checks, metrics, and event consumption from managed Kafka

solution.solutionTitle: GraphQL CRM + marketing API on hexagonal Spring Boot

solutionList.title: Single GraphQL entrypoint
solutionList.description: One schema spanning account, CRM, customer, and marketing bounded contexts at `/api/graphql`.

solutionList.title: Hexagonal / DDD modules
solutionList.description: Ports and adapters per domain — GraphQL in, JPA/Redis out — keeping business logic testable.

solutionList.title: JWT + Redis sessions
solutionList.description: Access/refresh tokens with session state stored in Redis (Upstash in AWS).

solutionList.title: Cloud-native deployment
solutionList.description: App on EC2, PostgreSQL on RDS, Redis on Upstash, Kafka consumed from a cloud broker; Actuator exposes Prometheus metrics.

metricsList: 750+ Java source files across CRM, marketing, account, and shared layers
GraphQL API prefix /api with context path and Actuator at /api/actuator/*
Deployed on AWS — EC2 application tier, RDS PostgreSQL, Upstash Redis
External observability via Spring Actuator (health, metrics, prometheus)

marketing (Campaign, Channel, Activity, Metric, Asset, AbTest, Attribution)

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal Architecture
designPatterns.description: Ports and adapters isolate domain from GraphQL, JPA, and Redis.

designPatterns.title: Domain-Driven Design
designPatterns.description: Bounded contexts for account, CRM, customer, and marketing.

designPatterns.title: CQRS (light)
designPatterns.description: Separate command and query types per use case without event sourcing.

designPatterns.title: Repository
designPatterns.description: Output ports abstract persistence behind adapter implementations.

designPatterns.title: Value Object
designPatterns.description: Immutable types (Email, DealStatus, UTMParameters, etc.) enforce invariants.

scalabilityStrategies.description: JWT + Redis sessions allow horizontal scaling of EC2 instances behind a load balancer.

securityStrategies.title: BCrypt passwords

securityStrategies.title: Rate limiting

decisions.title: GraphQL over REST
decisions.problem: Multiple CRM and marketing clients need flexible reads without version explosion.
decisions.solution: Single GraphQL schema with modular SDL and Spring GraphQL controllers.
alternatives: OpenAPI REST with many DTO permutations
decisions.outcome: One endpoint, strong typing, GraphiQL for developer experience.

decisions.title: Hexagonal modules per domain
decisions.problem: Marketing and CRM logic must stay testable as the system grows.
decisions.solution: package-by-domain with explicit ports (input/output) and adapters.
decisions.outcome: Clear boundaries; swap GraphQL or JPA without touching domain rules.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis instead of self-hosted on EC2
decisions.problem: Session and rate-limit state must survive app restarts and scale horizontally.
decisions.solution: Managed Upstash Redis with TLS; configured via SPRING_REDIS_HOST in production.
decisions.outcome: No Redis ops on EC2; suitable for minimalist deployment.

decisions.title: AWS RDS for PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Need durable relational storage with backups for CRM/marketing data.
decisions.solution: RDS PostgreSQL 16; Flyway migrations on startup.
decisions.outcome: Managed backups and patching; app connects via JDBC env vars.

app:
  environment:
    SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: docker
    SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL: jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB}
    SPRING_REDIS_HOST: redis
    DB_HOST: postgres

features.title: GraphQL API
features.description: Schema-first GraphQL with modular `.graphqls` files for account, CRM, customer, and marketing domains. Single endpoint at `/api/graphql` with GraphiQL in dev.
highlights: 22 schema files organized by bounded context
Global exception handler for GraphQL errors

features.title: JWT authentication & sessions
features.description: Dual-token JWT (access + refresh) with BCrypt passwords and Redis-backed session storage. Separate secrets for access and refresh tokens.
highlights: Access token default 15 minutes; refresh 30 days
GraphQL directives for authentication and roles

features.title: CRM domain
features.description: Sales pipeline management — companies, opportunities, deals, quotes, tasks, CRM interactions, and service packages.
highlights: Deal and quote workflows with line items
Task statistics and CRM interaction analytics

features.title: Marketing domain
features.description: Campaign management, channels, activities, metrics, assets, A/B tests, campaign interactions, and attribution tracking.
highlights: UTM and conversion tracking on interactions

features.title: GraphQL rate limiting
features.description: Annotation-driven rate limits with global and per-operation profiles, backed by Redis counters and exposed via response headers.

features.title: Redis sessions & cache
features.description: Auth sessions and rate-limit state in Redis. Production uses Upstash; local development uses Docker Redis or Upstash tunnel.
highlights: Caffeine in-process cache for app-level TTLs

features.title: Observability & health
features.description: Spring Boot Actuator with health probes, metrics, and Prometheus export for external monitoring on EC2.

features.title: Kafka event consumption
features.description: Consumes events from a cloud-managed Kafka cluster (AWS MSK or external broker). Bootstrap servers configured via environment — not hardcoded in repository.
highlights: Intended for async domain events and integrations

features.title: Docker local development
features.description: Multi-stage Dockerfile and two Compose files — full stack or infra-only — with explicit host overrides for container networking.

name: MindCare Hub API
description: REST API for small mental-health practices: patient onboarding, specialist scheduling, confidential medical records, billing, and notifications for psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented tools for small therapy practices
problemStatement.problemDescription: Independent psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists often juggle spreadsheets, paper notes, separate calendars, and ad-hoc billing. That creates scheduling conflicts, weak access control on sensitive clinical data, and no single view of a patient's care journey.
problemList: Double bookings and unclear specialist availability across the week
Medical notes stored without role-based confidentiality rules
Patients, staff, and specialists using different systems for appointments and invoices
Manual follow-up for activation emails, reminders, and payment status
No unified API for a future patient portal or clinic admin dashboard

solution.solutionTitle: One API built for mental-health workflows

solutionList.title: Role-based access for the whole clinic
solutionList.description: JWT authentication with user types (patient, specialist, staff, admin) and service-layer checks on appointments, records, and payments.

solutionList.title: Scheduling that respects clinic rules
solutionList.description: Appointments enforce clinic hours (09:00–19:00), advance booking windows, overlap detection, and specialist daily caps.

solutionList.title: Confidential medical records
solutionList.description: Records support standard, sensitive, and highly_sensitive levels; patients cannot read highly sensitive entries; specialists own their documentation.

solutionList.title: Billing aligned with real practices
solutionList.description: Bills, cash and bank-transfer payments, Stripe online payments, refunds, and insurance claims in one billing module.

solutionList.title: Production-ready container deploy
solutionList.description: Multi-stage Docker image, Gunicorn, health checks, and external PostgreSQL plus Redis for cache, rate limits, and Celery.

metricsList: 6 Django domain apps (users, specialists, appointments, medical, billing, notification)
REST API v2 under /api/v2/ (appointments router mounted at /appointments/)
129+ Python modules in apps/ excluding migrations and tests
Docker image Python 3.11 slim, Gunicorn 3 workers × 2 threads

scalabilityStrategies.title: Managed PostgreSQL

scalabilityStrategies.title: Redis for hot paths

scalabilityStrategies.title: Async notification delivery

techDecisions.title: Django monolith vs microservices
techDecisions.problem: Small therapy group needs fast delivery and simple ops, not distributed complexity.
techDecisions.solution: Single Django codebase with clear app boundaries and service classes; one Docker image on EC2.
techDecisions.outcome: Lower operational overhead; scale vertically and with extra EC2 instances first.

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL on RDS
techDecisions.problem: Relational data with strict integrity for appointments, records, and billing.
techDecisions.solution: Managed RDS PostgreSQL with connection pooling (CONN_MAX_AGE 600).
techDecisions.outcome: ACID transactions for clinical and financial data; dev uses SQLite in development.py.

techDecisions.title: SimpleJWT
techDecisions.problem: SPA/mobile clients need stateless auth for patients and specialists.
techDecisions.solution: JWT access + refresh with rotation; custom claims for user_type.
techDecisions.outcome: Works well for API-first clients; requires blacklist app for secure logout.

techDecisions.title: External Redis (Upstash)
techDecisions.problem: Rate limits and cache should survive API container restarts on EC2.
techDecisions.solution: django-redis to cloud Redis; compose file does not bundle Redis container.
techDecisions.outcome: Shared cache across multiple API instances behind ALB.

dockerFiles.description: Single-service compose file; PostgreSQL and Redis are external cloud instances, not containerized.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  web:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/dockerfile
    container_name: mindcare_api
    env_file: ../.env
    environment:
      DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: config.settings.production
      ENVIRONMENT: production
    ports:
      - "8001:8000"
    volumes:
      - logs_volume:/app/logs
    restart: unless-stopped
# Cloud: RDS PostgreSQL + Upstash Redis

dockerFiles.description: Python 3.11 slim bookworm; builder installs requirements; runtime runs as non-root django user.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM python:3.11-slim-bookworm AS builder
# gcc, libpq-dev, pip install -r requirements.txt
FROM python:3.11-slim-bookworm AS runtime
USER django
EXPOSE 8000
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "/app/scripts/entrypoint.sh"]
# Gunicorn: 3 workers, 2 threads, migrate on start

dockerFiles.description: Waits for DB_HOST/REDIS_HOST, runs migrate, optional superuser/dummy data, starts Gunicorn.
dockerFiles.content:
# nc -z DB_HOST DB_PORT  (PostgreSQL ready)
# nc -z REDIS_HOST REDIS_PORT  (Redis ready)
python manage.py migrate --noinput
gunicorn config.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:${PORT:-8000} --workers 3 --threads 2

name: Pimienta Alimentos Backend
description: Production Spring Boot API for Pimienta Alimentos — a real-world company operations platform used daily by employees and staff. Covers HR, payroll, inventory, CRM, contracts, tasks, and file management.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented operations for a growing food company
problemStatement.problemDescription: Pimienta Alimentos needed a single backend to coordinate employees, payroll, inventory, sales opportunities, contracts, and internal tasks across headquarters — replacing spreadsheets, ad-hoc tools, and disconnected processes that did not scale with daily staff operations.
problemList: Employee records, attendance, and work schedules lived in separate spreadsheets with no audit trail
Inventory movements (purchase, sale, transfer) lacked approval workflows and real-time stock visibility
Payroll periods and adjustments were error-prone without a centralized ledger
CRM opportunities and internal projects had no shared pipeline or milestone tracking
No secure, role-based API for the company frontend used by staff in the field and office
Bulk data updates (headquarters, tasks, payroll) required manual re-entry

solutionList.title: Modular bounded contexts
solutionList.description: Ten domain modules (account, employees, contract, CRM, task, headquarter, inventory, payroll, files, notification) each follow ports-and-adapters architecture.

solutionList.title: JWT authentication with admin approval
solutionList.description: Staff register and log in via JWT; new accounts require administrator approval before access. Refresh tokens stored in Redis (Upstash in production).

solutionList.title: Operational workflows
solutionList.description: Inventory transactions, contract renewals, opportunity pipeline (win/lose/reopen), attendance start/end workday, and payroll adjustments are first-class API workflows.

solutionList.title: Bulk XLSX import/export
solutionList.description: Apache POI powers spreadsheet sync for employees, opportunities, tasks, headquarters, and payroll — matching how operations teams already work.

solutionList.title: AWS-backed file storage
solutionList.description: Employee photos and file assets upload to S3 with presigned download URLs and server-side image resizing.

solutionList.title: Production-ready infrastructure
solutionList.description: Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis; Docker images built via multi-stage Maven pipeline.

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal architecture
designPatterns.description: Each module splits core (domain + application + ports) from adapters (web, persistence, external).

designPatterns.title: Ports and adapters
designPatterns.description: Input ports (use case interfaces) and output ports (repository, storage) keep domain free of framework code.

designPatterns.title: Repository pattern
designPatterns.description: JPA-backed repositories implement output ports; aggregates extend BaseDomain.

designPatterns.title: Domain events
designPatterns.description: e.g. AccountPendingApprovalEvent triggers notification listeners asynchronously.

designPatterns.title: Token bucket rate limiting
designPatterns.description: Global Redis filter plus per-controller @RateLimit profiles (STRICT, READ_HEAVY, etc.).

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API instances
scalabilityStrategies.description: JWT + Redis refresh tokens allow horizontal scaling of EC2/Docker replicas behind a load balancer (placeholder: ALB not yet documented in repo).

scalabilityStrategies.title: Managed RDS and Upstash
scalabilityStrategies.description: Database and cache are external to the container; API container is lightweight and replaceable.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Key-prefixed Redis
scalabilityStrategies.description: PIMIENTA_REDIS_KEY_PREFIX avoids collisions on shared Upstash instances.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Pagination defaults
scalabilityStrategies.description: PageableRequest caps page size at 100; PagedResponse standardizes list endpoints.

securityStrategies.title: JWT Bearer authentication
securityStrategies.description: 15-minute access tokens; 7-day refresh tokens stored in Redis; logout invalidates refresh.

securityStrategies.title: Role-based access
securityStrategies.description: ROLE_ADMIN and ROLE_MANAGER enforced in SecurityConfig and method security.

securityStrategies.title: Admin-approved registration
securityStrategies.description: New users start PENDING_APPROVAL until an administrator approves the account.

securityStrategies.title: Rate limiting
securityStrategies.description: Per-IP global bucket on /api/** plus stricter profiles on auth and sensitive operations.

securityStrategies.title: Production actuator lockdown
securityStrategies.description: application-prod.yaml exposes only health and info; metrics require ADMIN.

cacheStrategies.name: Refresh token store
cacheStrategies.description: Redis keys for JWT refresh sessions; invalidated on logout.

cacheStrategies.name: Global rate limit buckets
cacheStrategies.description: Per-IP token bucket via Lua script (ratelimit-token-bucket.lua).

cacheStrategies.name: Endpoint rate limits
cacheStrategies.description: @RateLimit profile-specific buckets per controller method.

decisions.title: Hexagonal modules over layered monolith
decisions.problem: Ten operational domains (HR, inventory, payroll, CRM) would tangle in a single package.
decisions.solution: Bounded contexts under module/* with core + adapter split and shared kernel (BaseDomain, pagination).
alternatives: Single-layered Spring package by technical concern
decisions.outcome: Clear ownership per domain; deployable monolith suitable for current team size and EC2 footprint.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over in-container Redis
decisions.problem: Production on EC2 needs durable cache/session store without co-locating Redis on the same VM.
decisions.solution: REDIS_URL with rediss:// to Upstash; key prefix for multi-tenant safety.
decisions.outcome: Managed TLS Redis for refresh tokens and rate limits with minimal ops overhead.

decisions.title: Flyway + validate ddl-auto
decisions.problem: Schema drift between environments risks production data corruption.
decisions.solution: Versioned migrations in db/migration; Hibernate validates entity mapping only.
decisions.outcome: Reproducible schema across local Docker, CI, and RDS.

decisions.title: JWT stateless sessions
decisions.problem: Staff clients need mobile-friendly auth without server-side HTTP sessions.
decisions.solution: Short-lived access JWT + Redis-backed refresh tokens; stateless SecurityFilterChain.
decisions.outcome: Scalable auth model aligned with SPA/frontend clients.

codeExamples.title: Stateless JWT security filter chain
codeExamples.description: Public paths for auth, health, and Swagger; role rules for admin/manager routes; JWT filter before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: Central security policy for the production API used by all staff clients.
files.content:
http.csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
    .sessionManagement(s -> s.sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS))
    .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
        .requestMatchers("/api/v1/auth/**").permitAll()
        .requestMatchers("/api/v2/health/**").permitAll()
        .requestMatchers("/api/v1/files/management/**").hasRole("ADMIN")
        .anyRequest().authenticated())
    .addFilterBefore(jwtAuthenticationFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class);

FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-25-alpine AS build

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage build from backend project root; non-root user; exposes 8080.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-25-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /workspace
COPY pom.xml .
COPY src ./src
RUN mvn -q -B -DskipTests package

FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
RUN addgroup -S spring -g 1001 && adduser -S spring -u 1001 -G spring
COPY --from=build /workspace/target/*.jar /app/app.jar
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs && chown -R spring:spring /app
USER spring:spring
EXPOSE 8080
ENV SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=docker
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "exec java ${JAVA_OPTS:-} -jar /app/app.jar"]

name: Restaurant Management API
description: Production REST API for restaurant operations — JWT auth, menu and stock, table seating, orders, payments with 16% VAT, and reservations. Deployed with AWS RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis (via REDIS_URL).

problemStatement.problemDescription: Small and mid-size restaurants often track menus, table availability, orders, inventory, and payments in separate tools or spreadsheets. That leads to double-booked tables, stock overselling, inconsistent billing, and no single API for a POS or admin dashboard.
problemList: Tables marked free while an active order is in progress
Menu changes not reflected consistently across ordering channels
Manual VAT and payment totals prone to error
Stock depletion discovered only after orders are placed
Reservation requests handled outside the order/table system

solution.solutionTitle: One API for front-of-house and back-of-house

solutionList.title: JWT-authenticated staff workflows
solutionList.description: Signup, login, logout, and logout-all via SimpleJWT with standardized JSON responses and 8-hour access tokens.

solutionList.title: Table-aware ordering
solutionList.description: Starting an order atomically marks the table unavailable; completing or cancelling releases it.

solutionList.title: Menu with cache-backed categories
solutionList.description: Public dish browsing with Upstash Redis-cached category lists and 15-minute HTTP cache on dish detail.

solutionList.title: Payments aligned to orders
solutionList.description: Payment records auto-created on order completion with 16% VAT via PaymentCalculatorService.

solutionList.title: Reservations with auto table assignment
solutionList.description: Customer reservation requests validate date, party size (max 8), and assign a suitable table when possible.

solutionList.title: Deployed cloud stack
solutionList.description: Dockerized Django API connected to AWS RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis for production cache.

metricsList: 9 Django domain apps (tables, users, menu, orders, payments, stock, reservations, authorization, shared)
Docker image Python 3.12 slim; docker-compose for local dev
DRF throttling 40/minute for anon and authenticated users
Redis cache timeout 600s default; menu categories cached 24h

techDecisions.title: AWS RDS + Upstash Redis (external managed services)
techDecisions.problem: Database and cache must survive API container restarts and scale independently.
techDecisions.solution: PostgreSQL via DJANGO_DB_* pointing to AWS RDS; cache via REDIS_URL to Upstash Redis DB 1.
alternatives: In-container PostgreSQL and Redis (docker-compose local only)
techDecisions.outcome: Production deploy uses managed RDS and Upstash; local dev uses docker-compose services.

techDecisions.title: Service layer over fat views
techDecisions.problem: Order/table/payment rules span multiple models and need transactional integrity.
techDecisions.solution: Dedicated *Service classes with atomic blocks called from thin views.
techDecisions.outcome: Testable business logic; clear separation between HTTP and domain rules.

techDecisions.title: django-redis over Memcached
techDecisions.problem: Need a shared cache compatible with cloud Redis and local docker-compose.
techDecisions.solution: Migrated to django-redis with REDIS_URL env var (Upstash in production).
techDecisions.outcome: Single REDIS_URL config works for Upstash, local Redis, and docker-compose.

codeExamples.title: Atomic table reservation on order start
codeExamples.description: OrderService.start_order validates table availability and marks the table taken inside a database transaction — preventing double seating.

files.name: order_service.py
files.explanation: Table availability check and atomic order creation with _take_table.
files.content:
if not table.is_available:
    raise TableNotAvailableForOrder(
        f"Table {table.number} is currently not available."
    )

with transaction.atomic():
    order = Order.objects.create(**validated_data)
    cls._take_table(order)

codeExamples.title: Auto payment creation on order completion
codeExamples.description: When staff completes an order, PaymentService generates line items from order items and calculates 16% VAT totals.

files.name: payment_service.py
files.explanation: Payment.from_order persists the record; ItemService and CalculatorService handle line items and VAT.
files.content:
payment = Payment.from_order(order)
payment.save()
payment_items = ItemService.generate_items_from_order(
    order.order_items.all(), payment
)
ItemService.save_items(payment_items)
CalculatorService.calculate_payment_totals(payment, payment_items)

dockerFiles.description: Production-ready API image — Python 3.12-slim, libpq-dev, installs requirements.txt, exposes 8000.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM python:3.12-slim
WORKDIR /restaurant_management_backend
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y libpq-dev gcc
COPY requirements.txt /restaurant_management_backend/
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . /restaurant_management_backend/
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["python3", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"]

dockerFiles.description: Three-service local stack — db, redis, restaurant_app on app_network.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:latest
    ports: ["5431:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:alpine
    ports: ["6379:6379"]
  restaurant_app:
    build: .
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    depends_on: [db, redis]
    environment:
      - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/1
      - DJANGO_DB_HOST=db
      - DJANGO_DB_NAME=mydb
      - DJANGO_DB_USER=postgres
      - DJANGO_DB_PASSWORD=postgres

name: Social Events API
description: REST API for discovering, hosting, and attending social events—organizations, ticket tiers, registrations, comments, in-app notifications, OAuth/JWT auth, and Celery-backed async work. Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented event management for communities and organizers
problemStatement.problemDescription: Community groups, venues, and independent organizers often rely on spreadsheets, generic form tools, and disconnected chat apps to publish events, sell or assign tickets, and keep attendees informed. That leads to double bookings, weak access control, no audit trail, and poor engagement after signup.
problemList: No single API for organizations to own events, members, and branding
Manual registration lists without capacity, waitlist, or check-in flows
Comments and notifications scattered outside the event domain
Authentication friction across web and mobile clients
Difficulty scaling traffic without caching and background email delivery

solution.solutionTitle: One API for the full event lifecycle

solutionList.title: Multi-tenant organizations
solutionList.description: Organizations with memberships, invites, join/leave, and org-scoped event listings for clubs and venues.

solutionList.title: Rich event domain
solutionList.description: Draft/publish/cancel, categories, tags, recurrence, staff roles (host, co-host, moderator), favorites, and personalized feeds.

solutionList.title: Registrations & ticketing
solutionList.description: Ticket tiers with pricing and capacity; registrations with pending, confirmed, waitlist, cancelled, attended states and host check-in.

solutionList.title: Engagement layer
solutionList.description: Threaded comments with likes and host pin/unpin; in-app notifications with read state and email templates.

solutionList.title: Cloud-ready operations

designPatterns.title: App per bounded context

designPatterns.title: Service layer (selective)
designPatterns.description: Notification and email logic lives in apps/notifications/services/; views stay thin with permission checks.

designPatterns.title: Custom permissions
designPatterns.description: Object-level permissions (IsEventHost, CanManageRegistrations) compose with organization membership.

designPatterns.title: Middleware cross-cutting
designPatterns.description: RateLimitMiddleware and AuditLogMiddleware wrap all requests before DRF.

designPatterns.title: Observer — signals

designPatterns.title: Soft delete base model

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API containers
scalabilityStrategies.description: Run multiple Gunicorn workers per EC2 instance; add EC2 instances behind ALB as traffic grows.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Amazon RDS
scalabilityStrategies.description: Managed PostgreSQL with backups; scale instance class or add read replica for reporting (placeholder).

scalabilityStrategies.title: Upstash Redis
scalabilityStrategies.description: Offload rate-limit counters and cache; prefix keys when sharing instance across projects.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Celery workers
scalabilityStrategies.description: Decouple email and long tasks from HTTP threads; scale workers independently on EC2.

scalabilityStrategies.title: S3 for media
scalabilityStrategies.description: Avoid filling EC2 disk—serve uploads from S3 when USE_S3 is enabled.

securityStrategies.title: JWT + per-view permissions
securityStrategies.description: Default authentication is JWT; each viewset declares IsAuthenticated, IsEventHost, or AllowAny explicitly.

securityStrategies.title: Production TLS & HSTS
securityStrategies.description: SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT, secure cookies, HSTS, and SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER in production.py.

securityStrategies.title: Audit logging
securityStrategies.description: AuditLogMiddleware with sanitized bodies; sensitive fields redacted.

securityStrategies.title: Layered throttling
securityStrategies.description: Global middleware plus DRF AuthActionsThrottle, RegistrationThrottle, CheckInThrottle.

securityStrategies.title: OAuth via allauth
securityStrategies.description: Social tokens handled by django-allauth; API returns JWT via custom oauth views.

decisions.title: Django monolith on EC2
decisions.problem: Need rapid feature delivery for event MVP without Kubernetes complexity.
decisions.solution: Single repo, Docker image, Gunicorn on EC2; domain split into Django apps.
decisions.outcome: Simple deploy path documented in docker/README.md; scale EC2/RDS vertically first.

decisions.title: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Relational model for organizations, events, registrations, and foreign keys.
decisions.solution: RDS in production; SQLite only in config.settings.development for local runserver.
decisions.outcome: CONN_MAX_AGE 600 reduces connection overhead from Docker web container.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis with key prefix
decisions.problem: One Redis account shared across multiple portfolio projects on Upstash.
decisions.solution: REDIS_KEY_PREFIX and Celery global_keyprefix social-events-api:.
decisions.outcome: No key collisions; rediss:// for TLS to Upstash endpoint.

decisions.title: SimpleJWT + allauth
decisions.problem: SPA needs stateless auth; users want Google/GitHub sign-in.
decisions.solution: JWT for API; allauth stores social accounts; custom views return tokens.
decisions.outcome: Consistent Bearer flow; blacklist on logout when token_blacklist enabled.

decisions.title: drf-spectacular for API contract
decisions.problem: Frontend and portfolio need discoverable endpoint documentation.
decisions.solution: Schema at /api/schema/, Swagger UI at /api/docs/.
decisions.outcome: Schema stays in sync with ViewSet decorators.

files.name: celery.py
files.explanation: Worker should run on EC2 alongside web container when email notifications must be reliable.
files.content:
app = Celery("social_events_api")
app.config_from_object("django.conf:settings", namespace="CELERY")
app.autodiscover_tasks()
app.autodiscover_tasks(["common"])
app.autodiscover_tasks(["apps"])

@task_prerun.connect
def task_prerun_handler(sender=None, task_id=None, task=None, **extra):
    logger.info(f"Task Started: {task.name} [ID: {task_id}]")

dockerFiles.description: Full local stack: Postgres, Redis, web, Nginx, Certbot on social_events_network.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    ports: ["5431:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports: ["6380:6379"]
  web:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
    environment:
      POSTGRES_HOST: postgres
      REDIS_HOST: redis
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/1
      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
  nginx:
    ports: ["80:80", "443:443"]

dockerFiles.description: Production profile: web container only; Postgres and Redis are external (RDS + Upstash).
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  web:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
    env_file: ../.env
    environment:
      DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: config.settings.production
    ports: ["${PORT:-8000}:8000"]
    volumes: [logs_volume, static_volume, media_volume]
# Cloud: RDS + Upstash configured in ../.env

dockerFiles.description: Waits for REDIS_HOST/POSTGRES_HOST, migrates, optional superuser/populate, starts Celery detached, runs Gunicorn.

name: Tourist Places API
description: REST API for discovering and managing tourist destinations: countries, categories, places, activities, reviews, and user-curated place lists—with JWT auth, Google OAuth2, and PostgreSQL on AWS RDS.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Scattered travel data with no unified API
problemStatement.problemDescription: Travel apps and content sites need a single backend to browse countries, categorize destinations, read reviews, and let users save personal place lists. Without a structured API, frontends duplicate validation, auth, and rating logic.
problemList: No shared catalog of countries, tourist places, categories, and activities
User-generated reviews and ratings stored inconsistently across clients
Account activation, password reset, and OAuth flows reimplemented per app
Hard to deploy consistently across environments without container + migration strategy
Public browse vs authenticated write operations need clear security boundaries

solution.solutionTitle: Modular Spring Boot REST API for tourist content

solutionList.title: Domain modules with clear boundaries
solutionList.description: Separate packages for auth, users, countries, places, activities, and reviews—each with controllers, services, DTOs, and repositories.

solutionList.title: JWT + Google OAuth2 authentication
solutionList.description: Email/password signup with activation tokens, refresh tokens, Redis-backed logout blacklist, and optional Google social login.

solutionList.title: Rich place discovery
solutionList.description: Search, filter by country/category, paginated listings, and user-curated place lists for trip planning.

solutionList.title: Schema-managed PostgreSQL
solutionList.description: Flyway migrations version the relational model; JPA validates against RDS in production.

solutionList.title: AWS-ready container deploy
solutionList.description: Docker image on EC2 connects to external RDS PostgreSQL and Redis/ElastiCache—no bundled database in compose.

metricsList: 6 domain modules (auth, user, country, places, activity, review)
Flyway V1 migration with 8 core tables

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API containers
scalabilityStrategies.description: Run multiple app containers on EC2 or scale to additional instances behind ALB; JWT avoids server sessions.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: Managed database with automated backups, Multi-AZ option, and storage autoscaling for growing place/review data.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Redis for shared token state
scalabilityStrategies.description: ElastiCache or Upstash lets multiple API instances share blacklist and activation token storage.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Caffeine for read-heavy country data
scalabilityStrategies.description: In-process cache reduces RDS queries on public country endpoints until cache invalidation strategy is needed.

securityStrategies.title: JWT Bearer authentication

securityStrategies.title: Redis token blacklist
securityStrategies.description: Logout and one-time tokens stored in Redis to prevent replay.

securityStrategies.title: Role model (VIEWER/EDITOR/ADMIN)
securityStrategies.description: Roles embedded in JWT claims; admin routes intended for elevated access—needs @PreAuthorize enforcement.

securityStrategies.title: CSRF disabled for REST
securityStrategies.description: Stateless API disables CSRF—acceptable for Bearer-token clients; not for cookie sessions.

securityStrategies.title: Flyway + ddl-auto validate
securityStrategies.description: Schema changes only via migrations; Hibernate validates entity mapping at startup.

decisions.title: Spring Boot monolith vs microservices
decisions.problem: Solo/small-team tourism API needs fast iteration and simple AWS ops.
decisions.solution: Single Spring Boot JAR in Docker on EC2 with package-per-domain modules.
decisions.outcome: One deploy artifact; scale EC2/RDS first before splitting services.

decisions.title: PostgreSQL on RDS
decisions.problem: Relational model with FKs between countries, places, reviews, and users.
decisions.solution: Flyway-managed schema on Amazon RDS PostgreSQL; H2 only in tests.
decisions.outcome: Strong integrity for ratings and place relationships; RDS handles backups.

decisions.title: JJWT + Redis blacklist
decisions.problem: SPA clients need stateless auth with logout support.
decisions.solution: Custom JwtService with Redis-backed blacklist instead of session cookies.
decisions.outcome: Works behind ALB; requires Redis availability in AWS.

decisions.title: Caffeine for countries
decisions.problem: Country list endpoints are read-heavy and change infrequently.
decisions.solution: In-process Caffeine cache with named caches per query pattern.
decisions.outcome: Simple and fast for single-instance EC2; consider Redis cache when horizontally scaling.

codeExamples.description: Defines public auth/swagger routes, permissive GET access, and JWT filter placement.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: CSRF disabled; stateless sessions; OAuth2 login for Google; review GET permitAll implications before prod.
files.content:
http.csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
    .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
        .requestMatchers("/signup", "/login", "/auth/**").permitAll()
        .requestMatchers("/v3/api-docs/**", "/swagger-ui/**").permitAll()
        .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.GET, "/**").permitAll()
        .anyRequest().authenticated())
    .sessionManagement(s -> s.sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS))
    .addFilterBefore(jwtAuthFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class);

dockerFiles.description: Cloud-first compose: app only; PostgreSQL commented out—use RDS. Redis configured via SPRING_DATA_REDIS_* env vars.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build: .
    container_name: tourist_places_app
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    env_file: .env
    environment:
      SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL: ${DB_URL}
      SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME: ${DB_USERNAME}
      SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    networks:
      - app-network
# Cloud: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL + ElastiCache Redis

dockerFiles.description: Maven 3.9 + Temurin 17 build stage; JRE 17 runtime with app.jar.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17 AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY pom.xml .
RUN mvn dependency:go-offline -B
COPY src ./src
RUN mvn clean package -DskipTests -B

FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /app/target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

name: Veterinary Clinic API
description: REST API for veterinary clinic operations—customer and pet onboarding, staff scheduling, clinical sessions (vaccinations, prescriptions, surgeries), payments, and multi-channel notifications. Built with hexagonal architecture, PostgreSQL (sqlc), and Redis-backed JWT sessions.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Disconnected tools for small and mid-size vet clinics
problemStatement.problemDescription: Veterinary practices often run reception desks, clinical records, pet owner portals, and billing on separate spreadsheets or legacy software. That leads to double bookings, lost vaccination history, weak access control on pet medical data, and no single API for a future mobile app or admin dashboard.
problemList: Staff cannot see a unified schedule across veterinarians and receptionists
Pet owners lack self-service booking and read-only access to their pets' clinical history
Clinical data (sessions, vaccines, prescriptions) scattered across paper or ad-hoc files
Payments and appointment status not linked in one transactional system
No standardized REST contract for integrators or a future client portal

solution.solutionTitle: One Go API for end-to-end clinic workflows

solutionList.title: JWT auth with role-aware routes
solutionList.description: Register, activate, login, refresh, logout, 2FA hooks, and password reset with tokens stored in Redis; Gin middleware enforces Bearer JWT on protected routes.

solutionList.title: Appointments with domain rules
solutionList.description: Customers request visits; employees manage assigned appointments; admins/managers search and operate clinic-wide—overlap detection (30 min buffer) and hourly capacity (max 5 concurrent).

solutionList.title: Customers, employees, and pets
solutionList.description: CRUD for clinic customers and staff profiles; pet records tied to customers with species/gender validation and soft-delete restore flows.

solutionList.title: Medical sessions (clinical module)
solutionList.description: Sessions with vaccinations, surgeries, prescriptions, attachments, services, and catalogs—customer read-only `/me/medical` vs staff write `/api/v2/medical` (service wiring in progress).

solutionList.title: Payments and notifications
solutionList.description: Payment lifecycle (create, process, cancel, refund) and notification inbox plus staff send/summary endpoints; email/SMS senders pluggable via env.

solutionList.title: Container-ready deploy
solutionList.description: Multi-stage Docker image, golang-migrate on startup, local full stack via `./docker/up-local.sh`, dev profile against cloud RDS + Upstash.

metricsList: REST API v2 prefix on most routes (`/api/v2/...`)
Roles: admin, customer, veterinarian, receptionist (JWT claim `role`)
Swagger annotations in handlers; UI when ENABLE_SWAGGER=true (non-production)

designPatterns.title: Hexagonal (ports & adapters)
designPatterns.description: Domain packages define interfaces; infrastructure implements repositories and HTTP handlers as driving/driven adapters.

designPatterns.title: CQRS-lite
designPatterns.description: Separate CommandService and QueryService types in appointments, payments, and users reduce coupling between reads and writes.

designPatterns.title: Specification pattern
designPatterns.description: Appointment and pet list endpoints build specification objects for composable SQL filters.

designPatterns.title: Domain service
designPatterns.description: AppointmentDomainService encapsulates overlap and capacity rules that need repository access.

designPatterns.title: DTO + mapper
designPatterns.description: HTTP DTOs in handlers/dtos map to domain commands via dedicated mapper structs.

designPatterns.title: Middleware pipeline
designPatterns.description: Recovery → CORS → AuditLog → RateLimiter → route groups with AuthMiddleware.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API tasks
scalabilityStrategies.description: Horizontally scale ECS tasks behind ALB; JWT validation requires no sticky sessions.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Managed PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: RDS handles backups and storage growth; connection pool tuned via DATABASE_MAX_* env vars.

scalabilityStrategies.title: External Redis
scalabilityStrategies.description: Upstash shared cache for tokens across multiple API replicas.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Read-heavy clinical queries
scalabilityStrategies.description: sqlc queries with pagination; add read replicas on RDS when reporting load grows.

securityStrategies.title: Bearer JWT on protected routes
securityStrategies.description: AuthMiddleware parses HS256 access tokens; role checked per route group.

securityStrategies.title: Customer data scoping
securityStrategies.description: Me/* routes resolve customer ID from JWT user ID before returning pets or medical data.

securityStrategies.title: Password hashing
securityStrategies.description: internal/shared/password encoder for stored credentials.

securityStrategies.title: Audit logging
securityStrategies.description: Request ID, method, path, status, and duration logged for traceability.

securityStrategies.title: Rate limiting
securityStrategies.description: Global IP-based limiter when RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true.

securityStrategies.title: RDS TLS
securityStrategies.description: database_url.go adds sslmode=require for Amazon RDS hostnames.

cacheStrategies.name: JWT / session tokens
cacheStrategies.description: TokenService stores access and refresh tokens in Redis when configured

cacheStrategies.name: Rate limit counters
cacheStrategies.description: In-memory sliding window per client IP in default middleware

cacheStrategies.name: PostgreSQL pool
cacheStrategies.description: pgx pool reuse with CONN_MAX_LIFETIME and idle timeouts

architectureFeatures.title: Unified APIResponse envelope
architectureFeatures.description: success, message, data, error, meta, timestamp, and request_id on every JSON response.

architectureFeatures.title: Swagger-first handlers

architectureFeatures.title: Domain modules per bounded context
architectureFeatures.description: Separate packages for auth, pets, medical, payments—clear boundaries for team ownership.

requestFlow.title: Client request
requestFlow.description: Client sends HTTP request; Bearer token on protected routes except public auth and /health.

requestFlow.title: Middleware chain
requestFlow.description: Recovery, CORS, audit log, optional rate limit, then JWT Authenticate and RequireAnyRole on route groups.

requestFlow.title: Handler → domain service
requestFlow.description: Gin handler validates DTO, maps to command/query, invokes core service.

requestFlow.title: sqlc repository
requestFlow.description: Persistence adapter runs parameterized SQL via pgx against RDS.

requestFlow.title: APIResponse JSON
requestFlow.description: shared/http helpers return success, pagination meta, or typed ApplicationError mapping.

eventFlow.title: Domain action
eventFlow.description: e.g. appointment confirmed or user registered triggers notification service call.

eventFlow.description: Row inserted in notifications table via sqlc repository.

eventFlow.title: Channel dispatch (when wired)

eventFlow.title: Client poll
eventFlow.description: User reads /api/v2/me/notifications for delivery status in MVP.

techDecisions.title: Go monolith vs microservices
techDecisions.problem: Solo/small team needs one deployable unit and strong typing without network chatter between services.
techDecisions.solution: Single module clinic-vet-api with hexagonal internal packages; scale ECS tasks horizontally.
techDecisions.outcome: Faster iteration; sqlc keeps SQL explicit and reviewable.

techDecisions.title: sqlc + pgx instead of ORM
techDecisions.problem: Complex clinical queries and performance predictability for appointment search.
techDecisions.solution: SQL files in db/queries generate type-safe Go; migrations in db/migrations.
techDecisions.outcome: Compile-time query checks; easy RDS tuning with raw SQL.

techDecisions.title: Gin over net/http alone
techDecisions.problem: Need routing groups, middleware, and fast JSON binding.
techDecisions.solution: Gin with validator integration and swagger-friendly handler structure.
techDecisions.outcome: Familiar ecosystem; middleware matches portfolio docs patterns.

techDecisions.title: Upstash Redis for tokens
techDecisions.problem: Multiple API replicas must share token revocation state.
techDecisions.solution: REDIS_URL with TLS (rediss://) for TokenService; local Redis in compose.local.
techDecisions.outcome: Matches serverless-friendly dev/prod parity; ElastiCache optional later in VPC.

techDecisions.title: Docker multi-stage Alpine
techDecisions.problem: Small attack surface image for ECS with migrations bundled.
techDecisions.solution: Builder compiles static binary; runtime runs entrypoint.sh + migrate + main.
techDecisions.outcome: ~minimal image with migration tooling included.

type APIResponse struct {
    Success   bool       `json:"success"`
    Data      any        `json:"data,omitempty"`
    Message   string     `json:"message,omitempty"`
    Error     *ErrorInfo `json:"error,omitempty"`
    Meta      any        `json:"meta,omitempty"`

    RequestID string     `json:"request_id,omitempty"`
}

codeExamples.title: Migrate-then-run container entrypoint
codeExamples.description: Production-friendly startup: wait for Postgres, run golang-migrate up, then exec the API binary.

files.name: entrypoint.sh
files.explanation: SKIP_MIGRATIONS and SKIP_DB_WAIT escape hatches for cloud dev profile.
files.content:
. /scripts/parse-database-url.sh
until pg_isready -h "$DB_HOST" -p "$DB_PORT" -U "$DB_USER"; do sleep 2; done
migrate -path ./db/migrations -database "$DATABASE_URL" up
exec "$@"

dockerFiles.description: Postgres 12, Redis 7, and API on vet_network. Started via ./docker/up-local.sh which rewrites DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL hostnames for in-network DNS.
dockerFiles.content:
name: vet-clinic-local
services:
  postgres12:
    image: postgres:12-alpine
    ports: ["5431:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    command: redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD}
  api:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile }
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: ${DOCKER_DATABASE_URL}
      REDIS_URL: ${DOCKER_REDIS_URL}
    depends_on: [postgres12, redis]

dockerFiles.description: Single API container pointing at cloud RDS and Upstash; SKIP_MIGRATIONS and SKIP_DB_WAIT default true for faster dev iteration.
Builder: Go 1.24 Alpine, compile main, install migrate CLI. Runtime: Alpine 3.18, non-root /root, EXPOSE 8000, entrypoint runs migrations then ./main.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM golang:1.24.0-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download && CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o main .
FROM alpine:3.18
COPY --from=builder /app/main .
COPY db/migrations ./db/migrations/
COPY scripts/entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
EXPOSE 8000
ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["./main"]

dockerFiles.description: Loads .env, parses DATABASE_URL via parse-database-url.sh, waits for Postgres (unless SKIP_DB_WAIT), runs migrate up, execs the API binary.
dockerFiles.content:
# Requires DATABASE_URL + DATABASE_USER + DATABASE_PASSWORD
# migrate -path ./db/migrations -database "$DATABASE_URL" up

httpEndpoints.description: Public liveness probe for ALB, Docker, and uptime monitors. Returns service name and version.
httpEndpoints.rateLimit: GLOBAL — RATE_LIMIT_RPS per IP when enabled

httpEndpoints.description: Creates admin, customer, or employee-linked user. Non-admin accounts may require email activation before login.

example.data: success registration. An Email will be sent to the user to activate their account.

httpEndpoints.description: Email/password login. Returns access and refresh tokens and user profile. May require 2FA verification when enabled on account.
httpEndpoints.rateLimit: GLOBAL — RATE_LIMIT_RPS per IP; lockout after MAX_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS

httpEndpoints.description: Activates pending user using user_id and activation code from email link (query or JSON body).
Issues new access token using valid refresh token. Requires Bearer authentication on refresh token flow per handler implementation.
Revokes refresh token for current device. Requires Authorization Bearer access token.
Revokes all refresh tokens for the authenticated user.
Returns profile for the authenticated user (any role).
Updates editable fields on the authenticated user's profile.
Paginated user search. Requires JWT roles admin or manager (see role alignment note in docs).
Admin creates a new user account with role assignment.
Admin/manager paginated customer search with filters in request body (POST-style search via GET handler binding).
Creates clinic customer record linked to user account when applicable.
Returns single customer for admin/manager.
Lists veterinary staff (veterinarians, receptionists) for scheduling and admin.
Registers new clinic employee profile.
Customer sees only their own appointment requests and bookings.
Customer requests a new visit; domain rules validate capacity and employee overlap when employee assigned.
Authenticated employee or manager sees appointments assigned to them.
Clinic-wide appointment search and filters for admin/manager roles.
Manager/admin confirms pending appointment. Optional query employee_id.
Cancels appointment with optional reason query param.
Authenticated users read their in-app notification inbox.
Employee/manager/admin manually triggers a notification to a user.
Staff search medical sessions. Requires medical handler wired in bootstrap (currently nil—endpoint inactive until wired).
Customer read-only view of clinical sessions for their pets.
